## Phase 1: Core Primitives (`garnet-common` + `tsavorite` foundations)

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/02, 04, 06, 15

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 1.1 | Implement `SpanByte` — variable-length byte sequence with 4-byte length prefix | DONE | 0.10 | Implemented in `garnet-common` as `SpanByte` packed header + `SpanByteRef`/`SpanByteRefMut` zero-copy views with safe `as_slice()` / `as_mut_slice()` wrappers and header bit validation. |
| 1.2 | Implement `RecordInfo` — 64-bit packed bitfield | DONE | 0.10 | Implemented as `#[repr(C)]` 8-byte packed header in `tsavorite` with previous-address masking, flag accessors/mutators, closed/scan semantics, and size/bitfield unit tests. |
| 1.3 | Implement `LightEpoch` — epoch protection with cache-line-padded table | DONE | 0.10 | Implemented `LightEpoch` with `#[repr(C, align(64))] EpochEntry`, per-thread pin/unpin guard API, global/safe epoch tracking, and deferred drain callbacks. |
| 1.4 | Implement `HashBucket` — 64-byte cache-line-aligned bucket with 7 entries + overflow | DONE | 0.10 | Implemented `#[repr(C, align(64))] HashBucket` with 7 data entries + overflow/latch word, overflow address helpers, and size/alignment tests (`64` bytes). |
| 1.5 | Implement `HashBucketEntry` — 8-byte packed entry (tag, address, tentative, pending) | DONE | 0.10 | Implemented atomic packed entry with tag/address/flag packing helpers, read-cache bit support, and CAS update APIs over `AtomicU64`. |
| 1.6 | Unit tests for all Phase 1 types | DONE | 1.1-1.5 | Added size/alignment and bitfield/behavior tests across Phase 1 primitives, including `proptest` roundtrip coverage for SpanByte serialization. |
| 1.7 | Benchmark Phase 1 hot-path operations | DONE | 1.6 | Added Criterion benchmark target (`phase1_hotpath`) in `tsavorite` for RecordInfo bit ops, HashBucketEntry CAS, and LightEpoch pin/unpin; benchmark target builds successfully. |

## Phase 2: Hybrid Log Allocator (`tsavorite`)

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/02, 06

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 2.1 | Implement page management — allocate/flush/evict pages, logical address space (page + offset) | DONE | 1.6 | Added `hybrid_log::PageManager` and `PageAddressSpace` with logical address encode/decode, page allocation, flush/eviction checks, and page-local read/write APIs plus lifecycle tests. |
| 2.2 | Implement log address pointers — TailAddress, ReadOnlyAddress, SafeReadOnlyAddress, HeadAddress, SafeHeadAddress, BeginAddress | DONE | 2.1 | Added `LogAddressPointers` with `AtomicU64` fields for all six pointers, monotonic shift APIs, tail `fetch_add`, and snapshot/test coverage for non-regressing pointer movement. |
| 2.3 | Implement lock-free tail allocation — `AtomicU64::fetch_add` for log append | DONE | 2.2 | Added `TailAllocator` with fetch-add reservation fast path, page-turn handling (`RetryNow`/`RetryLater`), sealed-page tracking, and page preallocation for next/next+1 pages. |
| 2.4 | Implement record format — RecordInfo + key SpanByte + value SpanByte, variable-length records | DONE | 1.1, 1.2, 2.1 | Added `hybrid_log::record_format` with size/layout computation, aligned serialization (`write_record`), and parse helpers for RecordInfo/key/value SpanByte views. |
| 2.5 | Implement page I/O — read/write pages to storage device abstraction | DONE | 2.1 | Added `PageDevice` trait (`read_async`/`write_async`), `InMemoryPageDevice`, and `flush_page_to_device` / `load_page_from_device` helpers with roundtrip + error-path tests. |
| 2.6 | Implement page eviction and read-from-disk path | DONE | 2.2, 2.5 | Added `page_residency` helpers for monotonic `HeadAddress` shift + flush/evict, plus disk fallback read path (`read_with_callback`) that loads pages when `address < HeadAddress`. |
| 2.7 | Unit tests for hybrid log allocator | DONE | 2.1-2.6 | Added allocator stress coverage: multi-page non-overlap checks and concurrent `TailAllocator` allocation uniqueness tests, alongside page residency read/eviction tests from 2.6. |

## Phase 3: Hash Index (`tsavorite`)

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/03, 06

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 3.1 | Implement hash index structure — power-of-2 bucket array, tag-based matching | DONE | 1.4, 1.5 | Added `HashIndex` with power-of-2 bucket allocation (`size_bits`), `hash -> (bucket_index, tag)` mapping, primary-bucket tag scan, and coverage tests. |
| 3.2 | Implement `FindTag` / `FindOrCreateTag` — CAS-based entry insertion | DONE | 3.1 | Added `HashIndex::find_tag` and CAS-based `find_or_create_tag` with tentative-slot install, duplicate-slot check, truncated-entry reclaim, and explicit `BucketFullNeedsOverflow` handoff for overflow path. |
| 3.3 | Implement overflow bucket allocation — fixed-page allocator for overflow chains | DONE | 3.1 | Added `OverflowBucketAllocator` with 64K-bucket fixed pages, lock-free free-list reuse (`SegQueue`), and integrated overflow-chain allocation/linking in `HashIndex::find_or_create_tag`. |
| 3.4 | Implement transient S/X locking — shared/exclusive latch on bucket overflow entry | DONE | 3.1 | Added `HashBucket` transient latch APIs: `try_acquire_shared_latch`, `try_acquire_exclusive_latch`, `try_promote_latch`, and corresponding release operations with bounded spin/drain behavior. |
| 3.5 | Unit tests for hash index | DONE | 3.1-3.4 | Added concurrent same-tag/different-tag insertion tests, overflow-chain reachability checks, and collision-path regression coverage for `find_or_create_tag` + `find_tag`. |

## Phase 4: CRUD Operations (`tsavorite`)

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/06 (especially §2.12), 10

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 4.1 | Define `ISessionFunctions` trait — generic callback interface | DONE | 2.4 | Added `session_functions` module with `ISessionFunctions` trait, operation info structs (`ReadInfo`, `UpsertInfo`, `RmwInfo`), `WriteReason`, and callback signatures for read/upsert/RMW paths. |
| 4.2 | Implement `Read` operation — hash lookup → chain traversal → reader callbacks | DONE | 3.2, 4.1 | Added `read_operation::read` with hash-entry address lookup, previous-address chain traversal, key match filtering, region-aware reader callback dispatch (`ConcurrentReader` vs `SingleReader`), closed/tombstone handling, and disk-resident page loading path. |
| 4.3 | Implement `Upsert` operation — hash lookup → IPU or RCU → CAS into chain | DONE | 3.2, 4.1 | Added `upsert_operation::upsert` with tag-head lookup, key-chain search, mutable-region IPU (same-size fast path), tail copy-update/insert append, and hash-entry address CAS replacement. |
| 4.4 | Implement `RMW` (Read-Modify-Write) — with fuzzy region RETRY_LATER | DONE | 4.2, 4.3 | Added `rmw_operation::rmw` with key-chain search, fuzzy-region `RetryLater` handling (`safe_read_only <= addr < read_only`), mutable-region IPU, and copy-update append + hash-head CAS fallback. |
| 4.5 | Implement `Delete` — tombstone creation | DONE | 4.3 | Added `delete_operation::delete` with mutable-region in-place tombstone path, immutable append-tombstone path (`previous_address` linked), and hash-head CAS update plus not-found/retry handling tests. |
| 4.6 | Implement `TsavoriteKV<K, V>` facade — public API wrapping all operations | DONE | 4.2-4.5 | Added `tsavorite_kv` module with generic `TsavoriteKV<K, V, D>`, `TsavoriteSession`, config/init error types, per-operation epoch pinning, hash-based key routing, pointer/head management helpers, and wrappers for read/upsert/rmw/delete. |
| 4.7 | Unit tests for CRUD operations | DONE | 4.2-4.5 | Added facade-level CRUD unit tests for single-thread roundtrip, fuzzy-region `RetryLater`, tombstone visibility, and multi-thread read/write stress through session API. |
| 4.8 | Integration test: end-to-end key-value store | DONE | 4.6 | Added `tsavorite/tests/crud_integration.rs` with end-to-end insert/read/delete verification for N keys and a concurrent insert/read/delete variant. |

## Phase 5: Network Layer (`garnet-server`, `garnet-common`)

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/07, 08, 09

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 5.1 | Implement TCP accept loop and connection handler | DONE | 0.10 | Added async TCP server in `garnet-server` using `tokio::net::TcpListener`, per-connection spawned handler tasks, shutdown-aware accept loop, connection/byte metrics, and unit tests for accept+shutdown behavior. |
| 5.2 | Implement `LimitedFixedBufferPool` — tiered pool with power-of-2 size classes | DONE | 0.10 | Added `limited_fixed_buffer_pool` module with power-of-two level mapping, bounded per-level queues (`ArrayQueue`), secure clear-on-return behavior, overflow-drop semantics, and concurrent unit tests. |
| 5.3 | Implement RESP protocol parser — zero-copy `&[u8]` based | DONE | 0.10 | Added `garnet-common::resp` parser (`parse_resp_command`) for `*<n>\r\n$<len>\r\n...` frames, returning zero-copy argument slices via caller-provided output storage, with partial-frame/error handling tests. |
| 5.4 | Implement `ArgSlice` — 12-byte pointer+length reference to RESP argument | DONE | 5.3 | Added packed 12-byte `ArgSlice` in `garnet-common` with pointer/length accessors, conversion from byte slices, unsafe view reconstruction, layout tests, and RESP parser output support (`parse_resp_command_arg_slices`). |
| 5.5 | Implement command dispatch — `match` on command byte pattern | DONE | 5.3 | Added `garnet-server::command_dispatch` with case-insensitive fast-path byte checks for GET/SET/DEL/INCR and fallback lookup for additional command names, including ArgSlice-based dispatch entrypoints and tests. |
| 5.6 | Implement request lifecycle — parse → dispatch → storage op → response write → send | DONE | 5.1-5.5, 4.6 | Added `request_lifecycle` module with command execution over shared `TsavoriteKV`, RESP response builders, and connection-loop integration that parses frames, dispatches commands, executes storage ops, writes responses, and handles partial frames/protocol errors. |
| 5.7 | Unit tests for RESP parser | DONE | 5.3 | Added parser coverage for valid/invalid/partial RESP frames plus property-based generated bulk-argument roundtrip tests (fuzz-style input diversity) in `garnet-common::resp` tests. |
| 5.8 | Unit tests for buffer pool | DONE | 5.2 | Added `LimitedFixedBufferPool` tests for allocation/reuse, clear-on-return behavior, level-full drop semantics, size-class mapping, and concurrent get/return capacity invariants. |

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |

| 6.1 | Implement `GET` command — full path from TCP to response | DONE | 5.6 | Wired GET through `request_lifecycle` and connection loop: RESP parse → dispatch → Tsavorite read → RESP bulk/null response, validated by TCP integration test. |
| 6.2 | Implement `SET` command — upsert path | DONE | 5.6 | Extended SET path with option parsing for `NX`/`XX` and `EX`/`PX`, conditional write behavior (`$-1` on unmet condition), expiration bookkeeping, and invalid option/expire-time error handling. |
| 6.3 | Implement `DEL` command — delete path | DONE | 5.6 | Added DEL execution path with tombstone-backed delete calls and integer reply count (`:n\r\n`) over TCP request lifecycle. |
| 6.4 | Implement `INCR`/`DECR` commands — RMW path with in-place update | DONE | 5.6 | Added INCR/DECR via Tsavorite RMW callbacks (integer parse/add with overflow checks), including error response on non-integer values and TCP integration coverage. |
| 6.5 | Implement `EXPIRE`/`TTL`/`PEXPIRE`/`PTTL` — expiration metadata | DONE | 6.2 | Added EXPIRE/TTL/PEXPIRE/PTTL/PERSIST handling, periodic background expiry sweep, `RecordInfo.HasExpiration` propagation on write paths, and in-record expiration timestamp storage via value-prefix metadata (`u64` unix-millis) with reader/updater decode/encode flow. |
| 6.6 | Implement `PING`/`ECHO`/`INFO`/`DBSIZE`/`COMMAND` — utility commands | DONE | 5.6 | Added utility command handlers in `RequestProcessor` with RESP responses for PING/ECHO and dynamic INFO/DBSIZE/COMMAND output, plus unit/TCP integration coverage. |
| 6.7 | Integration test: redis-cli compatibility | DONE | 6.1-6.6 | Added `garnet-server/tests/redis_cli_compat.rs` that boots the server, executes PING/SET/GET/INCR/DEL via `redis-cli`, and verifies compatibility outputs. |
| 6.8 | Benchmark: GET/SET throughput and latency | DONE | 6.1, 6.2 | Added Criterion benchmark target `garnet-server/benches/redis_command_path.rs` for request-processor GET/SET hot-path throughput; benchmark target builds with `cargo bench --no-run`. |

## Phase 8: Checkpointing & AOF

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/05, 14

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 8.1 | Implement checkpoint state machine — fold-over and snapshot modes | DONE | 4.6, 1.3 | Added `checkpoint_state_machine` module with fold-over/snapshot modes, tokenized REST→PREPARE→IN_PROGRESS→WAIT_FLUSH→PERSISTENCE_CALLBACK→REST transitions, transition validation, TsavoriteKV facade APIs, and safe-epoch-gated wait-flush progression tests. |
| 8.2 | Implement AOF writer — append-only log with TsavoriteLog | DONE | 2.5 | Added `aof_log` module with append-only writer (`AofWriter`) using length-prefixed operation records and configurable flush-by-operation-count policy, plus sync and unit coverage. |
| 8.3 | Implement AOF replay — recovery from AOF | DONE | 8.2 | Added tolerant AOF record replay and server-side replay helpers (`replay_aof_file` / `replay_aof_operations`) that parse RESP operation frames and apply them through `RequestProcessor`, with unit coverage for roundtrip recovery and invalid-frame handling. |
| 8.4 | Implement checkpoint + AOF coordination | DONE | 8.1, 8.2 | Added `CheckpointAofCoordinator`, AOF offset tracking (`AofWriter::current_offset`), and suffix compaction (`compact_aof_file`) to retain only post-checkpoint tail records, with coordinator/compaction test coverage. |
| 8.5 | Integration test: crash recovery | DONE | 8.4 | Added crash-recovery integration coverage in `garnet-server::aof_replay` that simulates checkpoint restore + compacted AOF tail replay (insert data → checkpoint → more writes/deletes → recover → verify final state). |

## Phase 9: Transactions

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/12

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 9.1 | Implement MULTI/EXEC/DISCARD — command queuing and atomic replay | DONE | 6.1-6.4 | Added connection-scoped transaction state in the TCP request loop: `MULTI` starts queueing, commands return `+QUEUED`, `EXEC` replays queued frames as RESP array items, and `DISCARD` clears the queue; includes error paths for nested MULTI and EXEC/DISCARD without MULTI. |
| 9.2 | Implement WATCH — optimistic locking via WatchVersionMap | DONE | 9.1 | Added a fixed-size watch-version map (`Vec<AtomicU64>`) in `RequestProcessor`, incremented on successful key mutations (including expiration-driven deletes). Added `WATCH`/`UNWATCH` command handling in connection state and EXEC watch-version validation (`*-1` on conflict). |
| 9.3 | Unit tests for transactions | DONE | 9.1, 9.2 | Extended TCP pipeline integration coverage with MULTI/EXEC/DISCARD flows, WATCH conflict abort, UNWATCH behavior, wrong-arity errors, and command-registry assertions for transaction commands. |

## Phase 10: Cluster

**Read before starting**: docs/reimplementation/13, 14

| # | Task | Status | Depends On | Notes |
|---|------|--------|------------|-------|
| 10.1 | Implement cluster config — 16384 hash slots, immutable copy-on-write config | DONE | 6.6 | Added `garnet-cluster` cluster-config primitives: 16,384-slot map, 3-byte `HashSlot` representation (`[u16 worker_id | u8 state]`), worker table with reserved/local IDs, copy-on-write mutation APIs (`set_slot_state`, `add_worker`, `set_local_worker_role`), and snapshot-safe publish/load wrapper (`ClusterConfigStore`). |
| 10.2 | Implement gossip protocol — failure-budget sampling | DONE | 10.1 | Added `garnet-cluster` sampling core + `GossipCoordinator` wrapper with pluggable transport (`GossipTransport`) and deterministic randomized sampling helper. Added `GossipEngine` stateful round runner plus async transport/runner counterparts (`AsyncGossipTransport`, `run_round_async`, `AsyncGossipEngine`), and a `ClusterManager` timer/shutdown loop wrapper for periodic gossip rounds. Added deterministic `ClusterConfig::merge_from` + `ClusterConfigStore::merge_publish` helpers (epoch-first, node-id tie-break), `ClusterManager::run_with_config_updates` for inbound merge application, and `InMemoryGossipTransport` for peer snapshot delivery over channels with end-to-end manager merge coverage. Added socket-level gossip snapshot codec (`encode/decode_cluster_config_snapshot`) and framed TCP transport/read path (`TcpGossipTransport`, `read_gossip_snapshot`) with roundtrip tests, plus manager-level TCP gossip delivery E2E coverage. Added `FailureDetector` driven by per-round failed-worker tracking for threshold-based failure signaling. |
| 10.3 | Implement replication — primary-replica sync via checkpoint + AOF | DONE | 8.4, 10.1 | Added `garnet-cluster` replication sync scaffolding (`ReplicationManager`) with full-vs-incremental planning based on retained AOF replay window and replica offsets, plus replica progress tracking helpers. Added sync + async transport orchestration (`ReplicationTransport`/`AsyncReplicationTransport`, `execute_sync`/`execute_sync_async`) and worker-tracked wrappers (`execute_sync_for_worker` / `execute_sync_for_worker_async`) that drive checkpoint send + AOF stream calls according to current replica progress. Added bulk per-primary orchestration (`sync_replicas_of_primary` / `sync_replicas_of_primary_async`) for one-shot synchronization of all matching replicas. Added in-memory channel transport (`ChannelReplicationTransport`), file-backed transport (`FileReplicationTransport`), and TCP socket transport (`TcpReplicationTransport`) for runtime/integration coverage. Added failover election/apply path (`plan_failover`, `execute_failover`, `ClusterConfig::apply_failover_plan`) that promotes the best replica by offset and atomically reassigns failed-primary slots in config snapshots. |
| 10.4 | Implement slot migration — MOVED/ASK redirections | DONE | 10.1 | Added key→slot hashing (`CRC16/XMODEM` with hash-tag handling), slot-route decision helpers (`Local`/`Moved`/`Ask`/`Unbound`), RESP-ready redirection string helpers, and `garnet-server` cluster-routing hooks (`run_with_cluster`, `run_with_shutdown_and_cluster_config`, `run_listener_with_shutdown_and_cluster`) that return MOVED/ASK/CLUSTERDOWN before command execution. Added CROSSSLOT validation for multi-key DEL/WATCH and ASKING one-shot bypass for ASK routes, plus cluster-aware transaction slot consistency checks (`CROSSSLOT` during queueing -> `EXECABORT` on EXEC). Updated ASKING semantics to consume on the immediate next command (including non-key commands) in cluster mode. Added live slot-transfer plumbing in `RequestProcessor` (`export_migration_entry` / `import_migration_entry` / `migrate_keys_to`) for string and object values, plus slot-scoped discovery/transfer APIs (`migration_keys_for_slot` / `migrate_slot_to`) to migrate keys by hash slot instead of explicit key lists. Added explicit `ClusterConfig` slot-migration state helpers (`begin_slot_migration_to` / `begin_slot_import_from` / `finalize_slot_migration`) to model migration lifecycle transitions without direct raw state writes. |
| 10.5 | Integration test: multi-node cluster | DONE | 10.1-10.4 | Added a 3-node `garnet-server` integration test that verifies cross-node MOVED routing and simulated failover redirection updates via runtime cluster-config publish. Added `ClusterConfig::take_over_slots_from_primary` helper to model replica takeover slot reassignment in config mutations, then replaced manual failover slot rewrites with replication-driven automatic election/application (`ReplicationManager::execute_failover`) in the integration path. Added `FailoverCoordinator` in `garnet-cluster` to execute/publish failover plans once per failed primary against `ClusterConfigStore`, and updated server integration failover flow to execute via this coordinator path. Added `ClusterFailoverController` to orchestrate replica pre-sync + idempotent failover publish in one API, including primary-worker-id batch handling helpers. Added `ClusterManager::run_with_config_updates_and_failover` to wire gossip failure detection and failover orchestration in one async loop, and validated end-to-end server redirection updates with this integrated loop. Added `run_listener_with_shutdown_and_cluster_with_processor` plus a 2-node integration test that performs ASK-stage live key migration and validates post-finalization MOVED ownership updates via slot-based `migrate_slot_to` transfer. Added `execute_live_slot_migration` orchestration helper to drive begin/import/migrate/finalize lifecycle end-to-end with dedicated integration tests, plus step-wise throttled execution (`execute_live_slot_migration_step`), single-slot async runner (`run_live_slot_migration_until_complete`), and multi-slot round-robin async runner (`run_live_slot_migrations_until_complete`) validated by incremental/finalization and shutdown tests. Added control-plane-oriented slot-detection helpers (`ClusterConfig::slots_in_state`, `slots_assigned_to_worker_in_state`, `detect_live_slot_migration_slots`) and detected-slot runners for both one-shot and background polling (`run_detected_live_slot_migrations_until_complete`, `run_detected_live_slot_migrations_until_shutdown`), including server E2E coverage and multi-cycle shutdown-loop tests. Added combined control-plane wiring entrypoints (`run_cluster_manager_with_config_updates_failover_and_detected_migrations`, `run_listener_with_cluster_control_plane`, `run_with_cluster_control_plane`) with integration plus listener-level control-plane error propagation coverage. |

Documented usage and tunables in `garnet-rs/benches/README.md`. |

Verified a real `./bench garnet --tcp ...` run with `memtier_benchmark` generated `bench.json` successfully. |
| 11.3 | Add Dragonfly execution path for `cache-benchmarks` on macOS | DONE | 11.2 | Added `garnet-rs/benches/cache_benchmarks_dragonfly_docker_wrapper.sh` to run Dragonfly via Docker with `cache-benchmarks`-compatible process naming and startup flags, and documented config usage in `garnet-rs/benches/README.md`. |

Result snapshot: Garnet higher throughput (SET `336k` vs `101k`, GET `280k` vs `93k`) and lower p99 latency (SET `1.399ms` vs `3.727ms`, GET `1.055ms` vs `3.407ms`) in this environment. |
| 11.8 | Re-run comparison with both servers in Docker (`dragonfly` vs `garnet-rs`) | DONE | 11.3 | Added `garnet-rs` Docker benchmark wrapper + Dockerfile and reran same-parameter comparison with both targets containerized. Result snapshot: Garnet ahead on throughput and p99 (`SET 107.6k vs 100.3k`, `GET 103.9k vs 86.2k`, `SET p99 3.439ms vs 4.047ms`, `GET p99 3.455ms vs 4.191ms`). |
| 11.9 | Capture local GET-only/SET-only hotspot framegraphs and document reproducible method | DONE | 11.8 | Added `garnet-rs/benches/local_hotspot_framegraph_macos.sh` and documented usage in `garnet-rs/benches/README.md`. Latest sample run: `/tmp/garnet-hotspots-20260220-091323` with hotspot summaries showing mutex wait as dominant (`GET 40.79%`, `SET 46.90%`) followed by socket send/recv. |
| 11.10 | Add debug/test concurrency instrumentation (lock-order checks + deterministic sync points) and start string-store lock striping | DONE | 11.9 | Added `debug_concurrency` module (`OrderedMutex`, `debug_sync_point!`, block/unblock/wait APIs), wired sync points into GET/SET/expire paths, and introduced shard-aware string-store locking (`GARNET_TSAVORITE_STRING_STORE_SHARDS`, default `1`) with a parallel cross-shard GET/SET unit test. Quick local A/B (`threads=8`, `conns=16`, `n=20000`): shard `8` vs `1` improved SET `313.8k -> 666.3k` ops/s and GET `256.8k -> 654.0k` ops/s; shard `16` regressed SET, so tuning is required. |
| 11.11 | Add automated shard-sweep benchmark for lock-striping mode | DONE | 11.10 | Added `garnet-rs/benches/sweep_string_store_shards_local.sh` to run repeatable local sweeps (`1/2/4/8/16/...`) with built-in memtier summary validation and CSV output. |
| 11.12 | Tune shard-count policy after sweep data and define default/heuristic | DONE | 11.11 | Added `sweep_string_store_policy_matrix_local.sh` and ran a broader matrix (`w1:1x4`, `w2:4x8`, `w3:8x16`, `requests=20000`, `shards=1/2/4/8/16`) with `owner_threads={0,16}`. Median geo-mean throughput selected shard `2` for both modes (`owner0`: `308652` ops/s median geo-mean, `owner16`: `323754`), while `8/16` remained regressive. Runtime policy now defaults to `GARNET_TSAVORITE_STRING_STORE_SHARDS=2` when unset, with a `1`-shard fallback only when `GARNET_STRING_OWNER_THREADS=1`. |
| 11.13 | Validate shard-regression hypothesis with hotspot framegraphs (`shards=1` vs `16`) | DONE | 11.11 | Captured local A/B via `local_hotspot_framegraph_macos.sh` (`THREADS=8`, `CONNS=16`, `REQ_PER_CLIENT=30000`): throughput regressed at shard `16` (`GET 444280 -> 261276`, `SET 286002 -> 217070` ops/s). Framegraph delta: mutex wait dropped (`GET 49.8% -> 32.3%`, `SET 56.2% -> 41.7%`) while `tsavorite::hash_index` lookup paths rose sharply (`GET find_tag_address 0.05% -> 22.18%`, `SET find_tag_entry/find_or_create 0.54% -> 18.61%`), matching the "over-sharding without owner-thread routing increases storage-path cost" hypothesis. |
| 11.14 | Scale string-store hash-index size by shard count to avoid over-provisioned per-shard index | DONE | 11.13 | Added `scale_hash_index_bits_for_shards` in `request_lifecycle` so each string-store shard scales `hash_index_size_bits` down by `ceil(log2(shards))` (clamped to `>=1`) while object-store index size is unchanged. Added unit test `scales_hash_index_bits_with_shard_count`. Quick local sweep after change (`threads=8`, `conns=16`, `requests=20000`): shard `1` SET/GET `322749/320911`; shard `16` `225855/276991` (still regressed vs shard `1`, but GET improved vs pre-change `257843`). |
| 11.15 | Shard string metadata locks (expiration + key registry) with store shard mapping | DONE | 11.14 | Replaced global `expirations`/`key_registry` mutexes with per-shard vectors (`string_expirations`, `string_key_registries`) and centralized metadata helpers (`track_string_key`, `set_string_expiration_deadline`, `remove_string_key_metadata`). Added regression test `sharded_string_metadata_tracks_keys_and_expiration_per_shard`. Full tests pass (`83 + 3 + 1`). Quick sweep (`threads=8`, `conns=16`, `requests=20000`): shard `1` SET/GET `322521/324126`; shard `16` `230558/273398` (global-lock contention reduced, but high-shard regression remains without owner routing). |
| 11.16 | Add owner-thread routing primitive (fiber-free shard-affine executor foundation) | DONE | 11.15 | Added `shard_owner_threads` module with `ShardOwnerThreadPool` (`shard -> owner thread` stable mapping, submit/sync execution API, graceful shutdown) and tests for mapping stability, per-shard thread affinity, and cross-owner parallel execution. This keeps Tokio/network unchanged and prepares the next step: moving string command execution onto shard-owner threads without custom fiber scheduler. |
| 11.17 | Fix hotspot capture fidelity by stopping macOS `sample` when benchmark ends | DONE | 11.13 | Updated `local_hotspot_framegraph_macos.sh` to run sampling in background and terminate it when memtier finishes, avoiding idle-tail stack pollution on short/fast runs. Documented behavior in `garnet-rs/benches/README.md`. |
| 11.18 | Integrate optional owner-thread routing for hot single-key string commands | DONE | 11.16 | Added optional routing in `handle_connection` gated by `GARNET_STRING_OWNER_THREADS` (clamped to shard count). Routed commands: `GET/SET/INCR/DECR/EXPIRE/PEXPIRE/TTL/PTTL/PERSIST` and single-key `DEL`. Added helper tests (`owner_routed_shard_selection_handles_single_and_multi_key_commands`, `execute_frame_via_processor_matches_direct_execution`) and verified compatibility path with `GARNET_STRING_OWNER_THREADS=2 cargo test -p garnet-server --test redis_cli_compat`. |
| 11.19 | Measure owner-thread routing sensitivity vs owner-thread count (local, shard=16) | DONE | 11.18 | Local sweep (`threads=8`, `conns=16`, `requests=20000`, `shards=16`) with `GARNET_STRING_OWNER_THREADS={0,4,8,16}`: `0 => SET/GET 224291/290076`, `4 => 231361/287302`, `8 => 235927/274015`, `16 => 257485/288272`. For low-contention `shards=1`, enabling owner routing showed small regression (`330746/330764` -> `320563/328595` with owners=8). Current position: keep owner routing opt-in; use for high-contention/high-shard experiments. |
| 11.20 | Run shard sweep under owner-thread routing to identify practical shard band | DONE | 11.19 | Local sweep (`threads=8`, `conns=16`, `requests=20000`, `GARNET_STRING_OWNER_THREADS=16`) over `shards=1/2/4/8/16`: `1 => 314106/333952`, `2 => 329537/333944`, `4 => 321031/335535`, `8 => 300223/329379`, `16 => 251922/287843` (SET/GET). Best observed band is `2-4` shards; high shard counts still regress materially. |
| 11.21 | Extend cache-bench wrappers to support owner-thread routing controls | DONE | 11.18 | Updated `cache_benchmarks_garnet_wrapper.sh` and `cache_benchmarks_garnet_docker_wrapper.sh` with `CACHE_BENCH_GARNET_OWNER_THREADS` / `GARNET_STRING_OWNER_THREADS` plus opt-in thread-hint auto-mapping (`CACHE_BENCH_GARNET_AUTO_OWNER_THREADS=1`). Updated benchmark README accordingly. |

| 11.28 | Raise server default hash-index sizing and validate with repeated A/B sweep | DONE | 11.27 | Updated server config derivation to use `DEFAULT_SERVER_HASH_INDEX_SIZE_BITS=16` (unless overridden by env) with new unit coverage in `request_lifecycle`. | 11.29 | Reduce repeated shard-slot hashing on `GET/SET` hot path | DONE | 11.28 | Added shard-index-aware fast paths (`expire_key_if_needed_in_shard`, shard-index lock/metadata helpers) so `GET/SET` compute shard mapping once per command in the common case. | 11.30 | Skip expiration-map lock on shards with no TTL keys (`GET/SET` fast path) | DONE | 11.29 | Added per-shard expiration counters in `RequestProcessor` and lock-avoidance checks in expiration lookup paths (`expire_key_if_needed_in_shard`, `string_expiration_deadline_in_shard`, `expire_stale_keys`). Added regression test `string_expiration_counts_stay_consistent_across_updates`; request-lifecycle suite now reports `running 42 tests` / `42 passed`. Local hotspot rerun (`/tmp/garnet-hotspots-post-expcount-20260220-131945`) was near-neutral vs prior run (`GET +0.72%`, `SET -0.59%`), so this is retained primarily as contention-avoidance groundwork. |
| 11.31 | Enable `TCP_NODELAY` for accepted client sockets and re-profile GET/SET single-command path | DONE | 11.30 | Updated accept loop to call `TcpStream::set_nodelay(true)` per connection. Local hotspot rerun (`/tmp/garnet-hotspots-post-nodelay-20260220-132224`) vs pre-nodelay showed `GET -0.09%`, `SET +1.07%`, with p99 improving (`GET -4.37%`, `SET -0.50%`) in this sample (`docs/performance/local-hotspot-macos-2026-02-20-expcount-nodelay.md`). |

| 11.37 | Add robust local binary A/B harness and propagate server-error fail-fast checks | DONE | 11.36 | Added `garnet-rs/benches/binary_ab_local.sh` to compare any two binaries through the median gate harness and emit `comparison.txt`. Manual A/B artifact `/tmp/garnet-rxbuf-ab-20260220-141251` confirmed the receive-buffer copy rewrite was net-negative (`delta_set_ops=-13.07%`, `delta_get_ops=-1.04%`), so the code change was dropped. |
| 11.38 | Reduce owner-thread routing overhead by removing frame reparse path | DONE | 11.37 | Updated owner-routed command execution in `handle_connection` to copy parsed args and call `RequestProcessor::execute` directly on owner threads, instead of copying full RESP frames and reparsing. Owner-routing A/B (`/tmp/garnet-owner2-ab-ownedargs-20260220-144824`, `RUNS=3`, `PIPELINE=32`, `shards=2`, `owner_threads=2`) showed net improvement vs previous path: `SET +0.14%`, `GET +1.72%`, `SET p99 -1.03%`, `GET p99 -13.83%`; owner-disabled smoke A/B stayed throughput-neutral (`/tmp/garnet-owner0-ab-ownedargs-smoke-20260220-145108`). |

Updated `tools/tla/run_tlc.sh` to pass `-nowarning` by default (`TLA_NOWARNING=0` opt-out). Implemented minimal code fix in `ClusterManager::run_with_config_updates_and_failover_report`: execute fallback failover round only when shutdown is observed and no rounds were reported yet (`reports.is_empty()`), preserving steady-state behavior. Added cluster regression `cluster_manager_failover_report_runs_round_on_empty_shutdown`, replaced time-based shutdown in `run_with_cluster_control_plane_binds_and_serves_requests` with oneshot synchronization after successful client `PING`, and confirmed stability with 10 consecutive reruns. | 11.386 | 5-agent code audit of all command handlers | DONE | — | Ran 5 SubAgents with different personas (Consistency, RESP Protocol, Error Handling, API Surface, Code Hygiene) across all command handler files. Identified ~30 findings; fixed highest-severity items in TODOs 11.379–11.385. | Added `CLIENT TRACKINGINFO`/`CLIENT GETREDIR` support in `connection_handler` path with per-connection tracking state (mode, redirect, bcast/prefixes, noloop, caching flag), expanded `CLIENT TRACKING` option parsing (`REDIRECT/BCAST/PREFIX/NOLOOP/OPTIN/OPTOUT`), fixed `CLIENT CACHING` to accept `YES/NO` semantics for OPTIN/OPTOUT, and updated CLIENT HELP surface accordingly. Also added `repl-min-slaves-to-write` to CONFIG SET integer validation plus regression tests. Validation: `make test-server` (`375 + 23 + 1` pass); compatibility report rerun reached step `[3/4]` and again stalled at `unit/pause` heartbeat (`ok=613 err=9 ignore=129 timeout=0`) before manual stop. |
| 11.390 | Reduce RESP integer/length formatting allocations in hot writers (`RT-002` slice 1) | DONE | 11.386 | Replaced `to_string().as_bytes()` in RESP writer helpers (`request_lifecycle/resp.rs`, `connection_handler.rs`) with stack decimal append paths; added edge regressions (`i64::MIN`, frame shape). Validation: `make test-server` (`380 + 23 + 1` pass). Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.390-resp-integer-format-allocation-cut/`. |
| 11.391 | Reduce replication-frame transient allocations in runtime rewrite/emit paths (`RT-002` slice 2) | DONE | 11.390 | Introduced borrowed-slice frame encoding and stack decimal rewrite in replication emit/rewrite paths (`publish_*`, `xreadgroup_replication_frames`, `replication_frame_for_command`, `rewrite_set/expire/getex/restore`) plus frame capacity pre-sizing. Validation: `make test-server` (`380 + 23 + 1` pass). A/B (`RUNS=5`) mixed (`ops` up, `SET p99` up), with explicit noise artifacts captured. |
| 11.392 | Re-benchmark `RT-002` slice 2 under replication-heavy workload and pin p99 impact | DONE | 11.391 | Completed dedicated primary+replica A/B with fixed parameters (write-heavy `1:0` + mixed `1:1`) using reproducible local runner (`tools/replication_ab_local.sh`). Two independent `RUNS=3` samples both showed positive movement (`SET/GET ops +8~16%`, p99 `-21~27%`) on the replication-heavy path. Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.392-replication-heavy-rebenchmark-rt002-s2/`. |
| 11.393 | Continue `RT-002` by removing remaining replication-parser allocation hotspots (`parse_resp_bulk_string`/`Vec` materialization) | DONE | 11.392 | Converted LMPOP replication parser (`parse_resp_bulk_string` / `parse_lmpop_replication_meta`) from owned `Vec` payload extraction to borrowed slices and added rewrite regression coverage (`replication_rewrites_lmpop_to_pop_with_count`). Validation: `make test-server` (`381 + 23 + 1` pass). Replication-heavy A/B showed mixed-path variance comparable to same-binary noise in this environment; kept change with explicit controlled revalidation follow-up. Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.393-lmpop-parser-allocation-cut/`. |
| 11.394 | Re-validate `11.393` under lower-noise controlled benchmark conditions (CPU pinning/isolated host load) | DONE | 11.393 | Executed controlled replication-heavy revalidation with lower-noise settings (`RUNS=7`, `THREADS=1`, `CONNS=2`, `REQUESTS=20000`, owner-thread pinning hint `CPU_SET=0`) and same-binary noise controls for both before/after binaries. Cross-binary mixed deltas (`ops -3.17%`, `SET p99 +12.70%`, `GET p99 +14.55%`) were partially within same-binary noise envelope (notably GET p99), so no confirmed path-specific regression signal. Kept `11.393` and added path-aligned follow-up benchmark task `11.395`. Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.394-controlled-revalidation-rt002-s3/`. |
| 11.395 | Add LMPOP-focused replication benchmark (path-aligned) and use it as `11.393` acceptance gate | DONE | 11.394 | Added path-aligned primary+replica LMPOP benchmark runner (`tools/lmpop_replication_ab_local.sh`) and executed controlled `RUNS=7` cross-binary + same-binary noise comparisons (`THREADS=1`, `CONNS=2`, `REQUESTS=20000`, owner-thread pinning hints). Final balanced `LPUSH:LMPOP=1:1` workload showed near-neutral cross-binary movement (`LMPOP ops +0.20%`, `LMPOP p99 +3.59%`) inside observed same-binary noise envelope (`p99 +5.03%` / `-32.47%`), so `11.393` is accepted/kept. Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.395-lmpop-focused-replication-gate/`. |
| 11.396 | Start `RT-001` with object-command hotspot baseline and benchmark design | DONE | 11.395 | Added reproducible object-mutation benchmark harness (`tools/object_mutation_ab_local.sh`) covering hash/list/set/zset mutation pairs with per-run preload and strict error-log rejection. Captured initial same-binary noise baseline (`RUNS=3`, `THREADS=1`, `CONNS=2`, `REQUESTS=8000`, `PRELOAD_REQUESTS=4096`) and documented baseline medians plus acceptance-gate draft in experiment report. Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.396-object-mutation-baseline-design/`. |
| 11.397 | Execute first `RT-001` redesign slice on hash mutation path and validate against `11.396` gate | DONE | 11.396 | Added single-field serialized-payload fast path for `HSET`/`HDEL` (when hash-field TTL metadata is absent), with codec helpers that update/delete fields without materializing full `BTreeMap` while preserving sorted field order and keyspace semantics. Validation: `make test-server` (`383 + 23 + 1` pass). A/B on `11.396` harness (`RUNS=3`, fixed params) shows strong hash-path gains (`HSET/HDEL ops +307.26%`, `p99 -79~-82%`) with non-target families near-neutral. Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.397-hash-single-field-payload-fast-path/`. |
| 11.398 | Port Redis long-chain `SRANDMEMBER` external case into Rust TCP test and profile set hot path | DONE | 11.397 | Added exact TCP integration test for Redis `tests/unit/type/set.tcl` long-chain scenario (`tests::srandmember_long_chain_external_scenario_runs_as_tcp_integration_test`) and removed the earlier minimized in-memory port. Added standalone external driver + macOS sample/flamegraph evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-08/srandmember-long-chain-profile/`. Before profile on the pre-rebuild release binary showed `handle_sadd -> load_set_object_payload -> decode_set_object_payload -> BTreeMap::insert` / `save_set_object -> serialize_set_object_payload` dominating. After rebuilding current source, the same driver completed with `create_seconds=3.810`, `total_seconds=14.012`, and the hotspot shifted to socket I/O / owner-thread handoff instead of repeated decode/save. Validation: `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`418 + 23 + 1`); `build_compatibility_report.sh` complete (`ok=1446 err=373 ignore=181 timeout=0` at `.../compatibility-report-20260308-232739/redis-runtest-external-full`). Note: the remaining full external failure for `SRANDMEMBER with a dict containing long chain` is now due to missing `DEBUG HTSTATS-KEY`, not the `SRANDMEMBER` path itself. |
| 11.398 | Expand `RT-001` hash fast path to multi-field mutation batches (`HSET`/`HDEL`) and re-validate | DONE | 11.397 | Added batch serialized-payload helpers for `HSET/HDEL` multi-field updates/deletes (duplicate-field semantics preserved), while keeping specialized single-field fast path to avoid regression. Validation: `make test-server` (`386 + 23 + 1` pass). Object-mutation A/B final rerun (`RUNS=3`, fixed params) vs `11.397`: hash near-neutral/improved (`HSET/HDEL ops +0.24%`, `p99 -7.48%/-4.49%`), non-target ops within `-1.12%`~`-0.26%`; captured initial regressed attempt and noisy intermediate rerun as separate artifacts. Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-04/11.398-hash-multi-field-payload-fast-path/`. |

| 11.503 | Implement real ACL semantics beyond the current minimal surface (`AUTH`/`ACL SETUSER`/channels/selectors/pubsub/subscriber state) | DONE | 11.442 | Runnable external-lane ACL parity is now closed. The final focused probe at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260316-010414/` reached `ok=105 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=4`, and the remaining `ignore=4` cases are upstream `external:skip` scenarios that require fresh-server / restart semantics rather than additional product work. The delivered scope covers ordinary Redis `AUTH`/`HELLO AUTH`, live ACL user state on `RequestProcessor`, password/hash/nopass rotation, command/key/channel/database enforcement, `ACL CAT` / `ACL DRYRUN` / canonical `ACL GETUSER` / `ACL LIST`, selector-aware authorization/rendering, live `ACL LOG` including `multi`/`lua`/`script` contexts, runtime config/file coupling (`acl-pubsub-default`, `aclfile`, `user`, `ACL SAVE`, `ACL LOAD`), pubsub subscriber revoke/recheck behavior, `EXEC`-time ACL revalidation, denied-command INFO counters, and the supporting external harness tightening required to verify those paths. |
| 11.504 | Design and implement OIDC JWT authentication / Workload Identity as an `AUTH` credential source | DONE | 11.503 | Added a first production-ready server-side OIDC/JWT `AUTH` slice on top of the completed ACL runtime. Best-fit crate selection was locked to `jsonwebtoken` `10.3.0` after current-source review versus `openidconnect`, `josekit`, and newer JWT alternatives, and the implementation now supports explicit token auth via `AUTH __OIDC__ <jwt>` and `HELLO ... AUTH __OIDC__ <jwt>` against configurable JWKS/issuer/audience/principal-claim/algorithm settings. JWT verification is file-backed JWKS for the first slice, rejects symmetric algorithms, requires existing enabled ACL users, resolves the authenticated user from the configured token claim, updates connection auth state to the resolved ACL principal, and reserves `__OIDC__` from normal ACL usernames to keep password auth and token auth unambiguous. Validation: targeted Rust regressions PASS (`oidc_auth_bearer_token_and_hello_auth_match_configured_jwks_scenarios`, `oidc_auth_config_validation_rejects_incomplete_or_unsafe_settings`, `acl_current_connection_and_hello_chain_match_external_scenarios`); `make fmt` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`670 passed; 0 failed; 4 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`); targeted external regression `RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/acl ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260316-022956/` (`failed_tests=0`, `ignore=8`). Follow-up topics for remote JWKS discovery/refresh and broader workload-identity rollout remain explicitly tracked in DeepResearch request `DR-007`. |
| 11.505 | Add downstream full-sync session/backlog groundwork so snapshot responses replay buffered post-cutover writes before live streaming | DONE | 11.504 / DR-008 / DR-010 | Reworked downstream replication handoff around explicit full-sync session state instead of `snapshot then raw subscribe`. `RedisReplicationCoordinator` now tags downstream write frames with replication offsets, retains a bounded replay backlog, captures a cutover offset for `PSYNC`/`SYNC`, replays buffered post-cutover frames after the initial sync payload, and then filters duplicate live frames before continuing on the broadcast stream. Control traffic such as `REPLCONF GETACK` is kept outside the replayed offset stream so ACK refreshes still reach live replicas without corrupting backlog replay. Added focused regressions for the new invariants (`prepare_downstream_fullresync_replays_only_post_cutover_frames`, `prepare_downstream_fullresync_skips_duplicate_live_frames_after_replay`). Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server prepare_downstream_fullresync -- --nocapture` => `2 passed; 0 failed`); `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`673 passed; 0 failed; 5 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`); targeted interop `garnet-rs/tests/interop/replication_capability_matrix.sh` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/replication-capability-20260316-044716/`, including `redis_primary_to_garnet_replica=PASS` and `garnet_primary_to_redis_replica=PASS`. This is correctness groundwork only: downstream export still emits the current empty RDB payload for real replicas until real non-empty snapshot export lands. |
| 11.506 | Add first real Redis-RDB full-sync slice for preexisting string datasets in both replication directions | DONE | 11.505 / DR-008 / DR-009 | Added the first non-empty Redis-RDB full-sync implementation for actual replication instead of command-stream-only bootstrap. `RequestProcessor` now builds a real `REDIS0011` snapshot for string keys across DBs with TTL preservation, validates it with `redis-check-rdb` in regression coverage when available, and can reload the same string-only RDB payload during upstream `FULLRESYNC` instead of discarding it. Downstream Redis replicas now receive seeded string datasets during initial sync, and Garnet-as-replica now materializes seeded string datasets from Redis primaries before continuing command streaming. Exact external Rust regressions were added for both directions (`garnet_primary_to_docker_redis_replica_receives_preexisting_string_dataset_on_full_sync`, `redis_primary_to_garnet_replica_receives_preexisting_string_dataset_on_full_sync`) plus local round-trip coverage (`redis_rdb_snapshot_round_trips_strings_ttls_and_nonzero_db`, seeded `SYNC` payload checks, `redis-check-rdb` validation). The remaining real-RDB gap is explicit: non-string object types are not yet supported in this first slice, so downstream export still falls back to the empty Redis payload for unsupported datasets and upstream ingest still rejects unsupported full-RDB payloads rather than silently loading partial state. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`sync_snapshot_includes_preexisting_string_dataset_for_real_replicas`, `sync_snapshot_rdb_payload_passes_redis_check_rdb_when_available`, `redis_rdb_snapshot_round_trips_strings_ttls_and_nonzero_db`); targeted exact external PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server garnet_primary_to_docker_redis_replica_receives_preexisting_string_dataset_on_full_sync -- --ignored --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server redis_primary_to_garnet_replica_receives_preexisting_string_dataset_on_full_sync -- --ignored --nocapture`); updated replication matrix PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/replication-capability-20260316-072618/`; `make fmt` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`676 passed; 0 failed; 7 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`). |
| 11.507 | Expand real Redis-RDB full-sync support to hash/set/zset seeded datasets in both replication directions | DONE | 11.506 / DR-009 | Generalized the real `REDIS0011` full-sync path from string-only entries to typed snapshot entries and added export/import support for plain `HASH`, `SET`, and `ZSET_2` payloads with key TTL and multi-DB preservation. `RequestProcessor::build_full_redis_rdb_snapshot` now emits real object snapshots for hashes (without field expirations), sets, and zsets instead of falling back to empty RDB, and upstream `FULLRESYNC` now reloads the same object families into Garnet. Added local round-trip coverage for hash/set/zset plus object TTLs, extended `redis-check-rdb` coverage to mixed datasets, and added exact ignored external integration tests for seeded object full sync in both directions (`garnet_primary_to_docker_redis_replica_receives_preexisting_object_dataset_on_full_sync`, `redis_primary_to_garnet_replica_receives_preexisting_object_dataset_on_full_sync`). Residual real-RDB gap intentionally kept: list/quicklist, stream, and compact upstream encodings (`*_LISTPACK`, `SET_INTSET`, `LIST_QUICKLIST_2`) are still unsupported in the real RDB path. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`redis_rdb_snapshot_round_trips_hashes_sets_zsets_and_object_ttls`, `sync_snapshot_rdb_payload_passes_redis_check_rdb_when_available`); targeted exact external PASS for both new object scenarios and both existing string seeded scenarios; updated replication matrix PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/replication-capability-20260316-073748/`; `make fmt` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`677 passed; 0 failed; 9 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`). |
| 11.508 | Add list seeded full-sync support plus default Redis 7.2 compact RDB ingest (`LIST_QUICKLIST_2`, `*_LISTPACK`, `SET_INTSET`) | DONE | 11.507 / DR-009 | Extended the real `REDIS0011` full-sync path to cover the default Redis 7.2 object encodings still missing from replication interop. `RequestProcessor::build_full_redis_rdb_snapshot` now exports canonical `LIST` entries, upstream `FULLRESYNC` now restores lists through `save_list_object(...)` so quicklist/listpack side state is rebuilt on import, and the full-RDB decoder now accepts upstream `LIST_QUICKLIST_2`, `HASH_LISTPACK`, `ZSET_LISTPACK`, `SET_LISTPACK`, and `SET_INTSET` payloads instead of failing closed on the default compact encodings. The exact upstream object full-sync regression no longer forces Redis away from compact encodings, and both object interop regressions now include a seeded list with a large middle element so the path exercises packed/plain quicklist nodes and list quicklist restoration. Added local regression `redis_rdb_snapshot_round_trips_lists_and_preserves_quicklist_encoding`, extended `sync_snapshot_rdb_payload_passes_redis_check_rdb_when_available` to validate list-bearing snapshots, and kept the existing seeded string exact tests green. Remaining explicit real-RDB gaps are stream export/import, legacy pre-listpack object encodings (`LIST_ZIPLIST`, `HASH_ZIPLIST`, `ZSET_ZIPLIST`, `LIST_QUICKLIST` v1), and hash field expiration RDB support. | 11.509 | Add seeded stream full-sync support to the real Redis-RDB path (`STREAM_LISTPACKS*` import, `STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` export, groups/PEL) | DONE | 11.508 / DR-009 | Extended the real `REDIS0011` full-sync path to preexisting stream datasets with consumer groups and pending state. `RequestProcessor::build_full_redis_rdb_snapshot` now exports `STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` payloads with stream entries, `last_generated_id`, `max_deleted_entry_id`, `entries_added`, consumer groups, global PEL, and consumer-local pending references; upstream `FULLRESYNC` now imports `STREAM_LISTPACKS`, `STREAM_LISTPACKS_2`, and `STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` into `StreamObject` instead of rejecting stream-bearing RDBs. Export is fail-closed for Garnet-only stream IDMP state/config that Redis RDB cannot represent. Remaining explicit real-RDB gaps are the legacy pre-listpack object encodings (`LIST_ZIPLIST`, `HASH_ZIPLIST`, `ZSET_ZIPLIST`, `LIST_QUICKLIST` v1) and hash field expiration RDB support. | 11.510 | Add legacy pre-listpack Redis-RDB ingest for full-sync replication (`LIST_ZIPLIST`, `HASH_ZIPLIST`, `ZSET_ZIPLIST`, `LIST_QUICKLIST` v1) | DONE | 11.509 / DR-009 | Closed the remaining legacy object-format gap in the real `REDIS0011` full-sync import path. `server_commands` now decodes legacy ziplist-backed `LIST_ZIPLIST`, `HASH_ZIPLIST`, `ZSET_ZIPLIST`, and v1 `LIST_QUICKLIST` payloads during upstream `FULLRESYNC`, converting them into Garnet list/hash/zset objects through the same typed snapshot reload flow used by newer listpack/quicklist2 formats. The in-tree decoder validates ziplist framing, entry chaining, and declared counts while preserving integer-as-ASCII semantics compatible with Redis/Valkey loaders. Added local exact regression `redis_rdb_snapshot_imports_legacy_ziplist_and_quicklist_encodings` using a handcrafted full RDB snapshot that exercises all four legacy formats, TTL preservation, large-entry quicklist restoration, and non-zero DB import. Added targeted ignored external interop `redis_6_2_primary_to_garnet_replica_receives_legacy_object_dataset_on_full_sync` to keep an old Redis primary full-sync scenario green alongside the existing seeded string/object/stream suites. The only explicit real-RDB replication gap now left is hash field expiration RDB support. | 11.511 | Add hash-field-expiration Redis-RDB support for full-sync replication and close the tracked real-RDB backlog | DONE | 11.510 / DR-009 | Closed the last tracked real-RDB full-sync gap by adding hash field expiration support to both export and import. `RequestProcessor::build_full_redis_rdb_snapshot` now snapshots per-field HFE metadata alongside hash payloads, emits Redis-compatible hash-with-metadata RDB records, and reloads them through the same typed snapshot path so imported hashes restore both object TTL and field-level expiration side state. The export header selection was corrected to match installed Redis 8.0.2 ground truth: HFE snapshots use `REDIS0012` rather than `REDIS0013`, which keeps `redis-check-rdb` and downstream Redis replicas compatible while still carrying `RDB_TYPE_HASH_METADATA` / `RDB_TYPE_HASH_LISTPACK_EX` payloads. Added local exact regression `redis_rdb_snapshot_round_trips_hash_field_expirations`, extended `sync_snapshot_rdb_payload_passes_redis_check_rdb_when_available` to validate HFE-bearing snapshots, and added exact ignored local-Redis interop tests in both directions (`garnet_primary_to_local_redis_replica_receives_hash_field_expiration_dataset_on_full_sync`, `local_redis_primary_to_garnet_replica_receives_hash_field_expiration_dataset_on_full_sync`). Also hardened the local Redis test harness so spawned `redis-server` instances use isolated temp dirs instead of inheriting any repo-root `dump.rdb`. This closes the tracked real-RDB replication backlog for the current Redis-compatible full-sync scope. Validation: `make fmt` PASS; targeted Rust PASS (`redis_rdb_snapshot_round_trips_hash_field_expirations`, `sync_snapshot_rdb_payload_passes_redis_check_rdb_when_available`); targeted ignored exact external/local PASS (`garnet_primary_to_local_redis_replica_receives_hash_field_expiration_dataset_on_full_sync`, `local_redis_primary_to_garnet_replica_receives_hash_field_expiration_dataset_on_full_sync`, `receives_preexisting_..._dataset_on_full_sync` suite, `garnet_primary_to_docker_redis_replica_receives_preexisting_object_dataset_on_full_sync` single rerun); replication matrix PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/replication-capability-20260316-102330/`; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`681 passed; 0 failed; 14 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`). |
| 11.512 | Enable standalone appendonly activation/load/rewrite path and close the tracked stubs (`CONFIG SET appendonly yes`, `DEBUG LOADAOF`, snapshot `BGREWRITEAOF`) | DONE | 11.511 / 795 | Replaced the standalone AOF compatibility stubs with a real file-backed path. `CONFIG SET appendonly yes` now rewrites a configured appendonly file from the current dataset, starts the live durability runtime, and rejects retargeting `dir` / `appendfilename` / `appendfsync` while AOF is enabled; `DEBUG LOADAOF` now flushes the in-memory dataset and replays the configured AOF source; and non-live `BGREWRITEAOF` now writes a real configured AOF snapshot instead of returning a stub. `LiveAofDurabilityRuntime` was made restartable/shutdown-aware and now uses current-Tokio spawn when available with a dedicated-thread fallback only for non-runtime callers, which preserves the existing `WAITAOF` timing semantics while still allowing `CONFIG SET appendonly yes` from owner-thread contexts. Added exact regressions for runtime activation / retarget rejection, live-write `DEBUG LOADAOF`, the existing stream/TTL AOF scenarios, and revalidated the affected `WAITAOF` cases. Remaining explicit standalone persistence gaps are boot-time automatic AOF replay and true live `BGREWRITEAOF` compaction / rotation while appendonly remains enabled. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`config_set_appendonly_yes_starts_runtime_and_rejects_retarget_while_enabled`, `config_set_appendonly_yes_persists_live_writes_for_debug_loadaof`, `idmp_and_xcfgset_persistence_match_external_stream_scenarios`, `expires_survive_debug_reload_and_loadaof_like_external_other_scenario`, `consumer_without_pel_is_present_after_aofrw_external_scenario_runs_as_tcp_integration_test`, `stream_aof_rewrite_after_xdel_lastid_matches_external_scenario`, `waitaof_replica_copy_before_fsync_like_external_wait_scenario`, `waitaof_master_isnt_configured_to_do_aof_like_external_wait_scenario`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`689 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Targeted external Tcl harness re-run was blocked by upstream Redis test-helper drift (`install_redis_external_pid_patch` could not match the current `tests/support/test.tcl`), so the affected Redis scenarios were verified through the exact in-repo TCP regressions that mirror those `unit/other` and `unit/type/stream` persistence cases. Smoke A/B at `/tmp/garnet-binary-ab-20260317-aof/comparison.txt` versus `HEAD` under `RUNS=3 THREADS=2 CONNS=4 REQUESTS=5000 PRELOAD_REQUESTS=5000 PIPELINE=1`: `SET ops +0.44%`, `GET ops -3.29%`, `SET p99 -16.75%`, `GET p99 +4.79%`. |
| 11.513 | Wire standalone server startup to replay configured appendonly file before serving traffic | DONE | 11.512 / 796 | `server_runtime` now replays the configured appendonly source before starting the live AOF runtime or accepting traffic, so a standalone boot with `appendonly yes` reconstructs dataset state from the on-disk AOF automatically instead of requiring `DEBUG LOADAOF`. Added exact TCP regression `startup_with_appendonly_replays_configured_aof_before_serving_traffic`, which proves `db0` + nonzero-DB recovery across two restarts and confirms post-replay live appends persist for the next boot as well. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server startup_with_appendonly_replays_configured_aof_before_serving_traffic -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server config_set_appendonly_yes_persists_live_writes_for_debug_loadaof -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`690 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`). No benchmark rerun for this slice because the change is boot-time only and does not alter steady-state request hot paths. |
| 11.514 | Implement real live `BGREWRITEAOF` compaction / rotation while appendonly is enabled | DONE | 11.513 / 797 | `BGREWRITEAOF` on a live appendonly server now performs a real rewrite/rotation instead of returning a compatibility reply after a drain wait. The live path temporarily quiesces write commands with an internal write-pause barrier, freezes active-expire cron, drains in-flight appends, materializes a rewritten snapshot AOF into a temp file, swaps it into place, and restarts the live appendonly runtime on the rewritten tail before unquiescing the server. Non-live `BGREWRITEAOF` keeps using the existing rewrite-to-temp path. Added exact TCP regression `live_bgrewriteaof_compacts_history_and_preserves_post_rewrite_writes_on_restart`, which verifies deleted keys are compacted out of the rewritten AOF and post-rewrite live writes still recover on the next boot; revalidated existing stream AOF rewrite regression coverage. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server live_bgrewriteaof_compacts_history_and_preserves_post_rewrite_writes_on_restart -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server stream_aof_rewrite_after_xdel_lastid_matches_external_scenario -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`691 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`). No benchmark rerun because the slice only touches the admin rewrite path and boot/runtime quiesce plumbing, not steady-state GET/SET hot paths. |
| 11.515 | Persist configured `dump.rdb` for `SAVE` / `BGSAVE` and teach `DEBUG RELOAD` to ingest either disk RDB or debug snapshot bytes | DONE | 11.514 / 795 | Closed the remaining standalone RDB persistence gap from the audit. `SAVE` and `BGSAVE` now materialize the configured snapshot to `dir/dbfilename` with an atomic temp-write + `sync_all` + rename flow instead of only caching an in-memory `saved_rdb_snapshot`. The snapshot builder prefers a real Redis-RDB export when the dataset is representable, but falls back to the existing debug-reload snapshot format for Garnet-only persistence state such as the stream `IDMP` / `XCFGSET` scenarios. `DEBUG RELOAD` source loading now first attempts the debug snapshot codec and then falls through to Redis-RDB ingest, so `DEBUG RELOAD` and `DEBUG RELOAD NOSAVE` work against either on-disk format. Added exact TCP regression `save_and_bgsave_persist_dump_file_and_debug_reload_nosave_uses_it`, and revalidated `bgsave_then_debug_reload_preserves_current_value_like_external_other_scenario` plus the in-memory stream fallback regression `idmp_and_xcfgset_persistence_match_external_stream_scenarios`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (the three regressions above); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`692 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `23 passed`, plus `1 passed`). No benchmark rerun because this slice only touches admin persistence / reload paths, not steady-state request hot paths. |
| 11.516 | Replay configured `dump.rdb` on standalone startup (with AOF precedence/fallback) and isolate test servers from stray local persistence files | DONE | 11.515 / 795 | Wired ordinary standalone boot to consume the configured dump snapshot file before serving traffic. Startup now loads `dir/dbfilename` when `appendonly no`, and when `appendonly yes` it still prefers the configured AOF if `appendfilename` exists but falls back to `dump.rdb` if no AOF source is present yet. The snapshot reload path reuses the same format-detection logic as `DEBUG RELOAD`, so startup accepts either Redis-RDB bytes or the existing debug snapshot bytes on disk. Because this made local stray `dump.rdb` / `appendonly.aof` files observable to server tests, the test startup path now redirects default persistence dirs to per-test temp roots under `cfg(test)` unless a test explicitly configured its own `dir`, keeping TCP/unit server tests deterministic without weakening real startup semantics. Added exact TCP regressions `startup_with_dump_snapshot_replays_before_serving_traffic` and `startup_with_appendonly_enabled_and_missing_aof_falls_back_to_dump_snapshot`, and revalidated the existing AOF boot regression plus representative contaminated tests (`tcp_pipeline_executes_basic_crud_commands`, `debug_populate_matches_external_redis_cli_rdb_dump_precondition`, `sync_rdb_dump_and_debug_reload_round_trip_smoke_in_nonzero_db`, `blocking_xread_streamid_edge_external_scenario_runs_as_tcp_integration_test`, `xreadgroup_dirty_semantics_match_stream_cgroups_external_scenarios`, `client_pause_write_keeps_randomkey_visible_until_unpause`). No benchmark rerun because the slice is boot/test-only and does not alter steady-state request hot paths. |

| 11.519 | Allow live `CONFIG SET appendfsync <policy>` while appendonly is enabled by making the local AOF runtime safely restartable | DONE | 11.518 | Closed the remaining appendonly reconfiguration gap that blocked Redis-style `WAITAOF` flows from switching `appendfsync` at runtime. `LiveAofDurabilityRuntime` now exposes shutdown completion waiting, and `stop_live_aof_durability_runtime()` drains already-published frames before restart instead of dropping the runtime handle before the append frontier can be observed. On the command path, `CONFIG SET appendfsync <always|everysec|no>` is now accepted while appendonly is enabled, triggering a safe runtime restart with the new fsync policy; `dir` and `appendfilename` retargeting remain rejected while AOF stays live. | 11.520 | Make `CONFIG SET appendfilename` immutable to match Redis/Valkey runtime config semantics | DONE | 11.519 | Tightened the appendonly config surface to match Redis/Valkey source semantics, where `appendfilename` is an immutable config. `CONFIG SET appendfilename ...` now returns the existing immutable-config error shape regardless of whether appendonly is disabled or currently live, while internal test/bootstrap code can still seed the configured filename through `set_config_value(...)`. | 11.521 | Add standalone boot-time persistence/ACL config overrides for rust server startup | DONE | 11.520 | Added single-port startup config plumbing for the rust server so boot-time persistence and ACL state no longer rely on runtime `CONFIG SET` or test-only `set_config_value(...)`. `ServerConfig` now carries typed `StartupConfigOverrides`, the launch parser accepts `GARNET_DIR`, `GARNET_DBFILENAME`, `GARNET_APPENDONLY`, `GARNET_APPENDFSYNC`, `GARNET_APPENDFILENAME`, and `GARNET_ACLFILE`, and startup applies those overrides before dump/AOF replay and eagerly loads the configured ACL file. To avoid unsafe path collisions, multi-port listener mode now rejects these overrides. Added exact regressions for launch parsing/validation and an end-to-end startup test proving `aclfile` load plus custom appendonly replay before serving traffic. | 11.524 | Promote config-derived path helpers to a crate-level module shared by runtime bootstrap and request lifecycle | DONE | 11.523 | Moved the shared config-path helpers from `request_lifecycle/config_paths.rs` into a new crate-level `src/config_paths.rs` so startup bootstrap, runtime config mutation, and admin persistence code all resolve `dir`/filename state through the same implementation. `request_lifecycle`, `server_commands`, and `server_runtime` now import the shared module, and startup `dir` normalization no longer carries a bootstrap-only helper that can drift from runtime behavior. No benchmark rerun because the slice is structural startup/admin cleanup and does not touch steady-state request hot paths. |
