| 11.538 | Cut owner-inline `SET` old-value materialization from the hot overwrite path and validate with wallclock/allocation flamegraphs | DONE | 11.537 | Narrowed the regression hunt from `11.537` to the specific `handle_set` overwrite path. `SET` now only materializes the previous string payload when semantics actually require it (`GET`, `IFEQ`, `IFNE`, digest equality/inequality conditions); the common overwrite path uses per-shard string/object key registries for existence checks and skips redundant string-registry reinsertion on overwrite. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server extended_set_conditions_match_external_string_scenarios -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server set_get_option_returns_old_value -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`703 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark/profile evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-19/11.538-handle-set-owner-inline-read-avoidance/`: wallclock owner-inline `SET` improved from `104667.38` to `109014.00 ops/sec` (`+4.15%`), `p99 0.103 -> 0.095 ms` (`-7.77%`), and heaptrack allocation capture dropped from `1362146` to `1313807` allocation calls (`-3.55%`) with peak live allocation `427.21K -> 249.12K` (`-41.69%`). |
| 11.539 | Add callback-bounded borrowed peek for compare-only reads and keep plain owner-inline `SET` on the fast path | DONE | 11.538 | Added a new borrowed-read API in `tsavorite` (`RecordView`, `PeekOperationStatus`, `TsavoriteSession::peek`) so storage clients can inspect a record inside a callback while the epoch is pinned, without materializing the value into an owned buffer first. `SET` compare-only conditions (`IFEQ`, `IFNE`, `IFDEQ`, `IFDNE`) now use that borrowed peek path, while `SET GET` intentionally remains on the owned-read path because the old value must outlive the callback. The first inline integration regressed plain overwrite `SET`, so the accepted design moved compare-only peek logic into a `#[cold] #[inline(never)]` helper and kept epoch scope callback-bounded instead of widening it to the whole command. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p tsavorite peek_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server extended_set_conditions_match_external_string_scenarios -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server set_get_option_returns_old_value -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`703 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark/profile evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-19/11.539-borrowed-peek-set-compare-cold-path/`: corrected wallclock owner-inline `SET` improved from `113349.02` to `119340.64 ops/sec` (`+5.29%`), `avg 0.03524 -> 0.03352 ms` (`-4.88%`), `p99 0.095 -> 0.087 ms` (`-8.42%`), `p99.99 0.551 -> 0.423 ms` (`-23.23%`), while heaptrack throughput moved from `1597.03` to `1642.07 ops/sec` (`+2.82%`) with allocation totals effectively flat (`2214936 -> 2214888`). |
| 11.540 | Skip `MONITOR` event formatting when no subscriber exists and re-benchmark the owner-inline hot path | DONE | 11.539 | Removed a clear source of unconditional per-command allocation in `connection_handler`: the server used to build `MONITOR` and Lua-monitor payloads for every command even when no client had subscribed to `MONITOR`. Added `ServerMetrics::has_monitor_subscribers()` and guarded monitor event formatting/publish behind it, then fixed the subscribed path with the exact TCP regression `monitor_receives_set_events_when_subscribed_like_external_scenario`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server monitor_receives_set_events_when_subscribed_like_external_scenario -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server acl_cluster_failover_monitor_and_shutdown_commands_cover_basic_shapes -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`704 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-19/11.540-monitor-no-subscriber-fast-path/`: final `RUNS=5` owner-inline A/B improved `SET ops 99828 -> 113049` (`+13.24%`), `GET ops 119189 -> 138964` (`+16.59%`), `SET p99 0.087 -> 0.079 ms` (`-9.20%`), and `GET p99 0.095 -> 0.071 ms` (`-25.26%`). |
| 11.541 | Reuse the per-client `last_command` buffer instead of rebuilding it on every command | DONE | 11.540 | `ServerMetrics::set_client_last_command(...)` used to allocate a fresh lowercase `Vec<u8>` for every command, including the common no-subcommand case. Reworked it to reuse the existing `client.last_command` buffer in place (`clear` + `reserve` + extend lowercase bytes), preserving the exact `cmd=` surface used by `CLIENT LIST`, `CLIENT INFO`, and ACL log payloads. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server client_info_and_list_follow_selected_db_and_reset_to_zero -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server acl_log_tracks_auth_and_acl_denials_like_external_scenarios -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`704 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-19/11.541-last-command-buffer-reuse/`: final `RUNS=5` owner-inline A/B improved `SET ops 117910 -> 120125` (`+1.88%`), `GET ops 135035 -> 136981` (`+1.44%`), with `SET p99` and `GET p99` flat at `0.071 ms`. |
| 11.542 | Remove per-command commandstats name allocation from the owner-inline hot path | DONE | 11.541 | `connection_handler` used to materialize a `Vec<u8>` commandstats name on every command before it knew whether ACL/error accounting would ever need that owned name, and `record_command_call(...)` lowercased into another fresh `Vec<u8>` before every stats update. This slice changed the hot path to record command calls from borrowed command/subcommand parts, using a fixed stack scratch buffer for common lowercase lookup hits and allocating only on first insertion or unusually long names; the combined `command|subcommand` buffer is now materialized lazily only when failure/rejection paths need it later in the request. Added exact regression `commandstats_normalize_subcommand_names_when_recorded_from_parts` and revalidated connection-path counting with `info_commandstats_counts_blocking_command_once` plus failure/rejection accounting with `consistent_eval_error_reporting_matches_external_scripting_scenario`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (those three tests); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`705 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-19/11.542-commandstats-lowercase-lookup/`: final `RUNS=5` owner-inline A/B improved `SET ops 117056 -> 122539` (`+4.68%`), `GET ops 138553 -> 141230` (`+1.93%`), `SET p99 0.079 -> 0.071 ms` (`-10.13%`), and `GET p99` stayed flat at `0.063 ms`. |
| 11.543 | Remove the unused owned upsert output clone from the owner-inline `SET` hot path | DONE | 11.542 | `handle_set` still paid for an owned `Output=Vec<u8>` upsert result even after the earlier read/peek cleanups, even though the common mutation path discards that output entirely. Added `KvWriteOnlySessionFunctions` with `Output=()`, kept the existing owned read/peek path for `SET GET` and compare-only conditions, and switched only the final string upsert stage to the write-only session so ordinary `SET` no longer clones the new stored value into an unused buffer. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server set_get_option_returns_old_value -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server extended_set_conditions_match_external_string_scenarios -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`705 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-22/11.543-write-only-set-upsert-output/`: the first identical `RUNS=5` owner-inline sample was noisy, so the accepted rerun is the second sample, which improved `SET ops 94773 -> 107351` (`+13.27%`), `GET ops 121842 -> 123988` (`+1.76%`), `SET p99 0.095 -> 0.087 ms` (`-8.42%`), and kept `GET p99` flat at `0.079 ms`. |
| 11.544 | Skip forced-raw string-encoding mutex work when the forced set is empty | DONE | 11.543 | Ordinary string writes still paid for `forced_raw_string_keys` mutex traffic even when no key was currently marked forced-raw, because `clear_forced_raw_string_encoding(...)` always locked and attempted a `remove(...)`. Added a small `forced_raw_string_keys_count` atomic in `DbCatalogSideState`, updated it only on real insert/remove transitions, and used it to short-circuit both `string_encoding_is_forced_raw(...)` and `clear_forced_raw_string_encoding(...)` when the forced set is empty. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server getbit_setbit_setrange_and_bitcount_follow_string_semantics -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server string_external_mutations_switch_integer_encoding_to_raw -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`705 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-22/11.544-forced-raw-empty-fast-path/`: final `RUNS=5` owner-inline A/B improved `SET ops 83286 -> 98684` (`+18.49%`), `GET ops 119429 -> 119624` (`+0.16%`), `SET p99 0.127 -> 0.111 ms` (`-12.60%`), and kept `GET p99` flat at `0.087 ms`. |
| 11.548 | Shard LRU/LFU key-access metadata with the string-store shard function | DONE | 11.544 | `record_key_access(...)` and the matching LFU/LRU helpers still funneled all plain `GET`/`SET` metadata traffic through one global mutex per map. This slice moved `key_lru_access_millis` and `key_lfu_frequency` to `PerShard<Mutex<...>>`, routed `record_key_access`, `clear_key_access`, `key_lru_millis`, `set_key_idle_seconds`, `set_key_frequency`, and `key_frequency` through `string_store_shard_index_for_key(...)`, and updated `SWAPDB` metadata swapping to touch every shard-local access map. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server db_scoped_object_access_metadata_does_not_leak_between_databases -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server swapdb_zero_and_nonzero_preserves_object_access_metadata -- --nocapture`); `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`705 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-22/11.548-per-shard-key-access-metadata/`: final `RUNS=5` owner-inline A/B improved `SET ops 99930 -> 102007` (`+2.08%`), `GET ops 107305 -> 112488` (`+4.83%`), `SET p99 0.127 -> 0.103 ms` (`-18.90%`), and `GET p99 0.103 -> 0.095 ms` (`-7.77%`). |
| 11.549 | Skip watch-version and tracking invalidation work when no watcher or tracking client exists | DONE | 11.548 | The third shortlist item targeted the remaining unconditional write-side bookkeeping around `WATCH` and server-assisted client tracking. Added a real connection-scoped watch registration guard so `watching_clients` is now accurate across `WATCH`, `UNWATCH`, `RESET`, `EXEC`/`DISCARD`, and disconnect, added a lightweight `tracking_clients` count for fast-path gating, and changed `bump_watch_version*_in_db(...)` to do nothing when both counts are zero, bump watch versions only when a watcher exists, and emit tracking invalidations only when a tracking client exists. Added exact TCP regression `info_clients_updates_watch_and_tracking_counts_on_disable_and_disconnect` and updated the internal DB-scoped watch-version regression to reflect the new invariant. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`info_clients_updates_watch_and_tracking_counts_on_disable_and_disconnect`, `watch_stale_key_then_lazy_delete_does_not_abort_exec`, `watch_versions_are_scoped_by_explicit_db`, plus the earlier DB-scoped access metadata regressions); `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`706 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-22/11.549-watch-tracking-fast-path/`: the first `RUNS=5` owner-inline A/B was mixed, so the accepted identical rerun is the second sample, which showed `SET ops 106971 -> 108149` (`+1.10%`), `GET ops 116096 -> 117511` (`+1.22%`), and flat `p99` on both `SET` and `GET` (`0.103/0.095 ms`). |
| 11.550 | Reintroduce borrowed-peek string reads only for the main-runtime `GET` / `STRLEN` / `GETRANGE` hot path | DONE | 11.549 | Retried the earlier borrowed-read idea in a much narrower form. Instead of widening the generic string read helpers, this slice added callback-bounded helpers in `string_commands.rs` only: `visible_string_user_value(...)` centralizes stored-value decode + expiration visibility, and `with_visible_main_runtime_string(...)` wraps `TsavoriteSession::peek(...)` for the main-runtime string store. `GET`, `STRLEN`, and `GETRANGE`/`SUBSTR` now use that helper to inspect borrowed bytes directly and avoid the intermediate `Vec<u8>` materialization that the regular `session.read(...)` path pays for. Auxiliary-db paths, wrongtype checks, and the generic string read helpers were left unchanged; the compare-only `SET` condition helper was also switched to the shared visibility helper so expiration semantics stay aligned. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server strlen_returns_zero_for_missing_and_length_for_string -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server getrange_and_substr_return_expected_slices -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server debug_sync_points_can_force_get_set_ordering_between_threads -- --nocapture`); `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`706 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-22/11.550-borrowed-peek-read-only-string-commands/`: final `RUNS=5` owner-inline A/B improved `SET ops 97469 -> 107032` (`+9.81%`), `GET ops 104751 -> 111228` (`+6.18%`), `SET p99 0.119 -> 0.103 ms` (`-13.45%`), and `GET p99 0.111 -> 0.095 ms` (`-14.41%`). |
| 11.552 | Replace `record_key_access` wall-clock reads with a monotonic access-metadata clock | DONE | 11.550 | `record_key_access(...)`, `key_idle_seconds(...)`, and `set_key_idle_seconds(...)` were still paying a `SystemTime -> UNIX_EPOCH` conversion on every plain `GET`/`SET` touch even though the hot-path requirement is only a monotonic access timestamp for `OBJECT IDLETIME`. This slice split access metadata off from TTL wall-clock semantics by adding a processor-local monotonic base (`access_clock_start`, `access_clock_start_unix_millis`) and routing LRU/idletime bookkeeping through `current_access_metadata_millis()`, which derives current access time from `current_instant()` plus the startup base. Exact regressions stayed green (`execute_with_client_no_touch_is_scoped_per_request`, `db_scoped_object_access_metadata_does_not_leak_between_databases`, `swapdb_zero_and_nonzero_preserves_object_access_metadata`, `object_idletime_returns_integer_for_existing_key`), `make fmt` and `make fmt-check` passed, and `make test-server` passed with verified counts (`706 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-22/11.552-monotonic-access-metadata-clock/`: owner-inline `RUNS=5` A/B improved `SET ops 73062 -> 76165` (`+4.25%`), `GET ops 87831 -> 93475` (`+6.43%`), kept `SET p99` flat at `0.175 ms`, and improved `GET p99 0.143 -> 0.127 ms` (`-11.19%`). |
| 11.554 | Cache slowlog threshold/max-len in atomics so the hot path does not lock config overrides | DONE | 11.552 | `record_slowlog_command(...)` was still taking the `config_overrides` mutex twice per command just to discover that the common request was below `slowlog-log-slower-than` and should not be logged. Added `slowlog_threshold_micros: AtomicI64` and `slowlog_max_len: AtomicUsize` to `RequestProcessor`, updated them from `set_config_value(...)`, and switched the slowlog fast path to atomic loads before any heavier slowlog work. Added exact regression `slowlog_runtime_config_cache_tracks_config_set_updates` to prove `CONFIG SET slowlog-log-slower-than` and `CONFIG SET slowlog-max-len` still reconfigure runtime behavior immediately. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server slowlog_runtime_config_cache_tracks_config_set_updates -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server slowlog_surface_validation_matches_external_scenarios -- --nocapture`); `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`707 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-23/11.554-slowlog-atomic-config-cache/`: the first `RUNS=5` owner-inline sample was much larger, so the accepted identical rerun is the second sample, which kept `SET` flat (`76392 -> 76235`, `-0.21%`), improved `GET` (`80963 -> 85710`, `+5.86%`), and reduced both p99s (`SET 0.167 -> 0.159 ms`, `GET 0.151 -> 0.143 ms`). |
| 11.555 | Add an unrestricted default-user ACL fast path for the plain hot path | DONE | 11.554 | Even with the default config, `acl_authorize_client_command(...)` was still taking two client-metrics locks plus a cloned ACL user profile lookup on every command before discovering that the authenticated client was using the unrestricted default user. Added `AclSelectorProfile::is_unrestricted_runtime()` and `AclUserProfile::is_unrestricted_runtime()`, cached that state for the default user in `RequestProcessor::default_acl_user_unrestricted`, updated the cache on ACL user/profile reloads, and collapsed client auth+user lookup to one `ServerMetrics::client_auth_state(...)` call. `acl_authorize_client_command(...)` now returns early for authenticated clients on the unrestricted default user while leaving restricted/default-modified and non-default users on the existing full ACL path. Added exact regression `default_acl_user_unrestricted_fast_path_tracks_profile_updates` and re-ran the targeted ACL suite (`cargo test -p garnet-server acl_ -- --nocapture`) before the full gate. Validation: `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`708 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-23/11.555-default-acl-fast-path/`: the first `RUNS=5` owner-inline sample was already positive, and the accepted identical rerun confirmed it with `SET ops 77011 -> 81836` (`+6.27%`), `GET ops 78947 -> 87654` (`+11.03%`), flat `SET p99` (`0.159 ms`), and `GET p99 0.159 -> 0.151 ms` (`-5.03%`). |
| 11.556 | Remove redundant client `last_activity` clock reads from internal bookkeeping setters | DONE | 11.555 | The next GET/SET flamegraph no longer pointed at string copies first; it showed `clock_gettime` in `ServerMetrics::set_client_wait_target_offset(...)` and other internal bookkeeping setters that still refreshed `last_activity` even though the same command already updated activity through input/output byte accounting. This slice keeps `last_activity` sourced by real socket activity (`add_client_input_bytes`, `add_client_output_bytes`) and removes the redundant `Instant::now()` calls from `set_client_last_command`, `add_client_commands_processed`, `set_client_wait_target_offset`, and `set_client_local_aof_wait_target_offset`. Added exact regression `client_last_activity_only_tracks_input_and_output_not_internal_bookkeeping` and re-ran the selected-db client surface test to keep `CLIENT INFO/LIST` semantics covered. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server client_last_activity_only_tracks_input_and_output_not_internal_bookkeeping -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server client_info_and_list_follow_selected_db_and_reset_to_zero -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`709 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.556-client-metrics-last-activity-fast-path/`: owner-inline `RUNS=5` A/B improved `SET ops 124345 -> 126536` (`+1.76%`) and `GET ops 143070 -> 147450` (`+3.06%`) while keeping both p99s flat at `0.071/0.063 ms`. |
| 11.557 | Skip rewriting `client.last_command` when the normalized command did not change | DONE | 11.556 | After `11.556`, the same client-metrics lane still paid lowercasing and buffer rewrite cost on every command even though memtier's hot path sends the same `GET` or `SET` over and over. Added `last_command_matches(...)` to compare the already-normalized `client.last_command` against the incoming command/subcommand without allocating, and changed `set_client_last_command(...)` to return early when the normalized bytes are unchanged. Added exact regression `client_last_command_stores_lowercased_command_and_subcommand` and kept the previous last-activity regression plus the selected-db `CLIENT INFO/LIST` integration regression in the targeted gate. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server client_last_activity_only_tracks_input_and_output_not_internal_bookkeeping -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server client_last_command_stores_lowercased_command_and_subcommand -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server client_info_and_list_follow_selected_db_and_reset_to_zero -- --nocapture`); `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`710 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.557-client-last-command-repeat-fast-path/`: the first owner-inline `RUNS=5` A/B was positive but small, so the accepted identical rerun is the second sample, which improved `SET ops 122725 -> 124947` (`+1.81%`), `GET ops 143344 -> 147005` (`+2.55%`), and reduced `SET p99 0.079 -> 0.071 ms` (`-10.13%`) while keeping `GET p99` flat at `0.063 ms`. |
| 11.559 | Replace reply-buffer settings mutex snapshots with atomics on the hot reply path | DONE | 11.557 | `add_client_output_bytes(...)` and `CLIENT INFO/LIST` still took a shared reply-buffer settings mutex on every reply render just to read `peak_reset_time`, `resizing_enabled`, and `copy_avoidance_enabled`, even though those values only change through rare DEBUG/config surfaces. Replaced `reply_buffer_settings: Mutex<ReplyBufferSettings>` with three atomics on `ServerMetrics`, kept the setter surface unchanged, and rebuilt `reply_buffer_settings_snapshot()` from relaxed loads. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server reply_buffer_limits_match_external_scenario -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`710 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.559-reply-buffer-atomic-snapshot/`: owner-inline `RUNS=5` A/B improved `SET ops 103509 -> 123025` (`+18.85%`) and `GET ops 117876 -> 145589` (`+23.51%`) with both p99s flat at `0.079/0.071 ms`. |
| 11.561 | Remove default-ACL hot-path user cloning when the client is still on authenticated `default` | DONE | 11.559 | Even after `11.555`, the unrestricted default-user fast path still paid one `client_auth_state()` lock plus `Vec<u8>` clone just to learn that the current client was authenticated as `default`. Added `ServerMetrics::client_is_authenticated_default_user(...)` so `acl_authorize_client_command(...)` can do a borrow-only check first when `default_acl_user_unrestricted` is true, and only fall back to the cloning snapshot path for non-default or restricted cases. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server default_acl_user_unrestricted_fast_path_tracks_profile_updates -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server acl_dryrun_and_cluster_saveconfig_countkeysinslot_getkeysinslot -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`710 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.561-default-acl-borrowed-check/`: the first owner-inline `RUNS=5` A/B was already positive, and the accepted identical rerun strengthened it to `SET ops 120554 -> 124898` (`+3.60%`), `GET ops 144245 -> 148533` (`+2.97%`), flat `SET p99` at `0.079 ms`, and `GET p99 0.071 -> 0.063 ms` (`-11.27%`). |
| 11.562 | Trial atomic top-level `commandstats` counting for dispatched hot-path commands | DONE | 11.561 | Tried moving top-level dispatched command counting off the shared `command_calls` mutex and onto an atomic array keyed by `CommandId`, while leaving subcommands and internal ad-hoc names on the existing lowercase map path. Exact regressions for merged `INFO COMMANDSTATS` snapshots and undo-on-blocking-ACL passed, and the full gate with the slice applied also passed (`712 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). The owner-inline benchmark did not converge to a clean win, though: three identical reruns under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.562-commandstats-atomic-top-level-drop/` flipped between `SET -6.45% / GET +4.13%`, then `SET +0.88% / GET +1.30%`, then `SET +5.63% / GET -4.10%`, with opposing p99 movement. Reverted the slice and kept only the experiment note. |
| 11.563 | Remove the per-command client-metrics auth snapshot from the plain ACL path by keeping connection-local auth state | DONE | 11.562 | Even after `11.561`, every ACL-checked command still took the `ServerMetrics.clients` mutex to snapshot `(authenticated, user)` before discovering that the current connection had not changed auth state since the previous request. Added `authenticated` and `authenticated_user` to `ClientConnectionState`, initialized and reset them from the default-user startup semantics, and synchronized them from metrics only on successful `AUTH` and `HELLO ... AUTH` side effects so password auth and OIDC both preserve the exact authenticated principal. Replaced `acl_authorize_client_command(...)` with `acl_authorize_connection_command(...)`, and threaded the same local auth snapshot through blocking-command and queued-transaction ACL checks. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`auth_side_effects_sync_connection_local_auth_state_from_metrics`, `reset_clears_multi_and_authenticated_state`, `acl_auth_password_rotation_and_hello_auth_match_external_scenarios`, `acl_current_connection_and_hello_chain_match_external_scenarios`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`711 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.563-connection-local-acl-fast-path/`: the short owner-inline `RUNS=5 REQUESTS=30000` reruns were noisy, so acceptance is based on the longer stabilization rerun with `REQUESTS=50000`, which improved `SET ops 108992 -> 124147` (`+13.90%`), `GET ops 136829 -> 144001` (`+5.24%`), `SET p99 0.087 -> 0.079 ms` (`-9.20%`), and `GET p99 0.095 -> 0.071 ms` (`-25.26%`). |
| 11.564 | Trial combining finalize-side client metrics updates into one lock-taking helper | DONE | 11.563 | Tried a narrower follow-up to the earlier dropped client-metrics coalescing slice by touching only the finalize path: `add_client_output_bytes(...)` and `add_client_commands_processed(...)` were temporarily merged into one `record_client_command_completion(...)` helper, and the single finalize-path call site in `connection_handler` was rewired to use it. Exact regressions for activity semantics, the new combined-helper behavior, and reply-buffer accounting all passed, and the full gate with the slice applied also passed (`712 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). The benchmark did not justify keeping it, though: the first owner-inline `RUNS=5` A/B under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.564-combined-finalize-metrics-update-drop/` regressed immediately with `SET ops 105613 -> 85523` (`-19.02%`), `GET ops 140352 -> 123100` (`-12.29%`), `SET p99 0.087 -> 0.199 ms` (`+128.74%`), and `GET p99 0.079 -> 0.087 ms` (`+10.13%`). Reverted the code and kept only the experiment note. |
| 11.565 | Localize read-tracking enablement to connections and remove the global tracking-client atomic from the read path | DONE | 11.564 | Non-mutating commands still called `track_read_key_for_current_client(...)`, which first loaded the global `tracking_clients` atomic even though the connection itself already knows whether `CLIENT TRACKING` / `CLIENT CACHING` currently make read tracking possible. Added `ClientConnectionState::should_track_reads()`, threaded that derived flag through owner-routing, blocking execution, and transaction execution into new `RequestProcessor` entry points, and changed the request context to preserve the connection-level tracking decision while mutating commands still disable read tracking. That made it possible to remove the redundant `has_tracking_clients()` check from the hot read path without changing `BCAST` or `OPTIN/OPTOUT` semantics. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`client_tracking_optin_only_tracks_reads_after_caching_yes`, `tracking_gets_notification_of_expired_keys_like_external_scenario`, `execute_owned_frame_args_via_processor_matches_direct_execution`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`712 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.565-connection-local-tracking-read-fast-path/` is mixed but acceptable for this small low-risk cleanup: three identical owner-inline long reruns (`RUNS=5 REQUESTS=50000`) landed at `SET/GET +7.66%/+3.20%`, then `+1.95%/-6.47%`, then `+4.98%/+0.34%`, with exact tracking semantics preserved and two of the three reruns staying net positive. |
| 11.583 | Combine input-byte and last-command client metrics updates under one lock in the plain request loop | DONE | 11.576 | The next small metrics/bookkeeping cut targeted the request-start side of the hot path. The connection loop still called `add_client_input_bytes(...)` and `set_client_last_command(...)` back-to-back, which meant two `clients` lock acquisitions against the same `ClientInfo` entry on every request. This slice adds `add_client_input_bytes_and_last_command(...)`, keeps the existing repeated-command fast path, and updates the hot request loop to perform input-byte accounting, `last_activity`, and normalized command bookkeeping in one critical section. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server combined_input_and_last_command_updates_counters_activity_and_lowercased_command -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server client_last_activity_only_tracks_input_and_output_not_internal_bookkeeping -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server client_last_command_stores_lowercased_command_and_subcommand -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server executes_set_then_get_roundtrip -- --nocapture`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`713 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-24/11.583-combined-input-and-last-command-metrics/`: two identical owner-inline `RUNS=5 REQUESTS=50000 PIPELINE=1` reruns both stayed net positive, landing at run 1 `SET +1.29%`, `GET +4.84%`, `GET p99 -10.13%`, and run 2 `SET +2.90%`, `GET +3.34%`, with flat `SET p99` on both runs. |
| 11.593 | Scope request-time snapshots to actual command execution and reuse them from scripts/owner routing | DONE | 11.592 / 11.579 | Revisited the earlier dropped request-time idea with a tighter correctness boundary. Added `RequestTimeSnapshot { instant, unix_micros }`, request-local helpers (`current_request_instant`, `current_request_time_snapshot`), and `RequestTimeSnapshotScope`, but only enter that scope around actual command execution on the main processor / owner-routing path. `execute_with_client_tracking_context_and_effects_in_db(...)` now lazily captures a request-time snapshot only when one is not already present, script `frozen_time` now reuses the ambient request snapshot when available, and client/runtime activity bookkeeping reads that shared `Instant` instead of calling `Instant::now()` independently on the hot request path. A broader connection-loop / blocking-loop / `EXEC`-wide scope was tried and rejected because it broke the exact replication lazy-expire scenario `sync_replication_stream_exec_preserves_expire_then_del_for_expired_blocked_list_key`, so the accepted design keeps frozen time bounded to real command execution only. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`request_time_snapshot_scope_reuses_fixed_time_within_request_helpers`, `request_time_snapshot_scope_enter_if_missing_with_is_lazy_when_scope_exists`, `scripting_time_command_uses_cached_time_like_external_scenario`, `xreadgroup_claim_with_two_blocked_clients_external_scenario_runs_as_tcp_integration_test`, `sync_replication_stream_exec_preserves_expire_then_del_for_expired_blocked_list_key`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`716 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-26/11.593-request-time-snapshot-command-scope/`: the narrow owner-inline single-thread `PIPELINE=1` shape stayed neutral (`SET -0.51%`, `GET -0.05%`, flat p99), while the multi-thread-client `PIPELINE=4` shape improved cleanly (`SET +3.27%`, `GET +5.05%`, `SET p99 -4.32%`, `GET p99 -4.08%`). |
| 11.595 | Replace non-blocking slowlog `Instant::elapsed()` timing with cached monotonic micros | DONE | 11.593 | Took the next evidence-backed clock cut after `11.593`. Fresh Linux perf on the multithread-client `PIPELINE=4` owner-inline shape still showed `__kernel_clock_gettime` as the top GET hotspot, and the stack pointed directly at `std::time::Instant::elapsed()` inside `RequestProcessor::execute_bytes_in_db(...)` for non-blocking slowlog timing. Added a process-global `CachedMonotonicClock` refreshed by a lightweight background thread, switched the non-blocking slowlog path to `cached_monotonic_micros()` start/end deltas via `record_slowlog_command_duration_micros(...)`, and kept the blocking/unblock slowlog path on the existing exact `Instant::elapsed()` code so the compatibility-sensitive blocking tests stay on the precise path. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`slowlog_threshold_and_entry_shape_match_external_scenarios`, `slowlog_rewritten_and_blocking_commands_match_external_scenarios`, `executes_set_then_get_roundtrip`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`716 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark acceptance bar under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-26/11.595-cached-slowlog-duration-clock/`: owner-inline `PIPELINE=1` was a small throughput win but slightly worse `GET p99` (`SET +1.65%`, `GET +1.63%`, flat `SET p99`, `GET p99 +10.13%`), while the pressure shape that motivated the slice improved cleanly (`THREADS=8 CONNS=16 PIPELINE=4`: `SET +6.43%`, `GET +2.24%`, `SET p99 -8.70%`, `GET p99 -2.06%`). The confirming Linux perf refresh also showed the hotspot shift we wanted: pre-change GET topped at `__kernel_clock_gettime 20.50%` with `Instant::elapsed 16.03%`, while post-change GET no longer had `Instant::elapsed` near the top and instead shifted to `cfree 16.89%`, `__wake_up_sync_key 15.82%`, and `HashIndex::find_tag_address 13.00%`. |
| 11.596 | Introduce explicit `CommandContext` for hot string commands and scripting entry time | DONE | 11.595 | Took the next step after `11.595`: move from ambient request-time access toward explicit command-scoped time. Added `CommandContext { time_snapshot }` at the dispatch boundary, threaded it through hot string command handlers (`GET`, `SET`, `SETEX`, `SETNX`, `PSETEX`, `GETSET`, `GETDEL`), routed `record_key_access(...)` through `ctx.instant`, and passed the same `ctx.time_snapshot` into scripting entry so script frozen time uses the command context instead of re-reading ambient request time. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`request_time_snapshot_scope_reuses_fixed_time_within_request_helpers`, `scripting_time_command_uses_cached_time_like_external_scenario`, `sync_replication_stream_exec_preserves_expire_then_del_for_expired_blocked_list_key`, `executes_set_then_get_roundtrip`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`716 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-26/11.596-command-context-hot-path-first-slice/`: owner-inline `PIPELINE=1` was effectively flat to slightly worse on GET (`SET +0.84%`, `GET -1.75%`, flat `SET p99`, `GET p99 +10.13%`), but the multithread-client pressure shape improved (`THREADS=8 CONNS=16 PIPELINE=4`: `SET +1.05%`, `GET +3.58%`, `SET p99 +0.76%`, `GET p99 -3.08%`). Kept because the shape we are actively chasing stayed positive and the code path is cleaner: command start time is now explicit in the hot dispatch and scripting entry path. |
| 11.597 | Finish `CommandContext` rollout across the full request-lifecycle command surface | DONE | 11.596 | Completed the structural follow-up requested after `11.596`: every `handle_*` command method in `request_lifecycle.rs` and `request_lifecycle/*_commands.rs` now takes an explicit `ctx`, dispatch passes `ctx` to the full command surface, and internal command-to-command calls carry the same `ctx`. The hot string paths and scripting entry still use `ctx` for real time-sensitive work, while leaf handlers that do not need request time yet take `_ctx` explicitly so the surface is uniform. Validation: `make fmt` PASS; targeted Rust PASS (`executes_set_then_get_roundtrip`); `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`716 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Surface audit under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-26/11.597-full-command-context-surface/` confirms `dispatch_missing_ctx=0`. Benchmark evidence was mixed on the narrow shape but positive on the pressure shape: owner-inline `PIPELINE=1` first run `SET -6.25%`, `GET +3.60%`, `GET p99 -9.20%`; rerun `SET -1.32%`, `GET +2.42%`, `SET p99 +10.13%`; multithread-client `THREADS=8 CONNS=16 PIPELINE=4` improved to `SET +6.67%`, `GET +2.68%`, `SET p99 -7.43%`, `GET p99 -1.44%`. Kept because the rollout objective is structural, the pressure shape improved clearly, and the narrow shape did not show a stable cliff on rerun. |
| 11.598 | Route `RequestTimeSnapshot` capture through the cached request clock and remove per-request hot-path clock syscalls | DONE | 11.597 | The code already had a 1ms `garnet-cached-clock`, but it only fed slowlog-style monotonic accounting while `RequestTimeSnapshot::capture()` still called `Instant::now()` and `SystemTime::now()` for each request. Promoted that clock into a shared `CachedRequestClock` in `lib.rs`, so `RequestTimeSnapshot::capture()` now returns a cached `{ instant, unix_micros }` snapshot, `current_request_activity_instant()` uses the same source when no request scope is present, and `current_instant()` falls back to the cached request snapshot instead of a fresh syscall. Added an exact regression `command_context_reuses_request_time_snapshot_when_present` to pin the command/request-time contract. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`command_context_reuses_request_time_snapshot_when_present`); `make fmt` PASS; `make fmt-check` PASS; `make test-server` PASS with verified counts (`717 passed; 0 failed; 16 ignored`, plus `26 passed`, plus `1 passed`). Benchmark evidence under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-26/11.598-cached-request-clock-for-command-context/` stayed mixed on the narrow owner-inline shape but positive on the multithread pressure shape: `PIPELINE=1` run 1 `SET +0.52%`, `GET -2.84%`, `SET p99 -7.77%`, `GET p99 +16.84%`; rerun `SET +6.93%`, `GET -3.57%`, `SET p99 -7.77%`, `GET p99 -7.77%`; `THREADS=8 CONNS=16 PIPELINE=4` improved to `SET +5.68%`, `GET +2.98%`, `SET p99 -9.40%`, `GET p99 -3.92%`. Kept because the hot pressure path improved, the narrow shape did not show a stable SET cliff, and command-scoped time no longer pays two fresh clock syscalls at request entry. |
| 11.599 | Re-measure multithread `PIPELINE=4` Garnet vs Dragonfly after cached request clock and refresh flamegraphs | DONE | 11.598 | Ran the same matched Docker/Linux `THREADS=8 CONNS=16 PIPELINE=4` single-lane-server comparison used in `11.592`, but after `11.598` landed. The headline result did not flip: Dragonfly is still ahead in this shape, with Garnet at `SET 229134.67 ops/sec`, `GET 337783.99 ops/sec`, versus Dragonfly `SET 453371.05`, `GET 481831.29`, and Garnet `SET/GET p99` at `2.23404/1.51505 ms` versus Dragonfly `1.12855/1.06109 ms`. But the gap narrowed materially relative to `11.592`, and the perf topology changed exactly as intended: Garnet `GET` no longer tops out on `__kernel_clock_gettime`; the new top sampled symbol is `14.71% __wake_up_sync_key`, while `__kernel_clock_gettime` fell to `0.54%` on `GET` and `0.27%` on `SET`. Measurement artifacts and flamegraphs are stored under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-26/11.599-cached-clock-flamegraph-refresh/`. This was a docs-only measurement iteration with no additional code changes or test rerun after `11.598`. |
| 11.419 | Triage post-commit full compatibility residuals (`err=66`, `ignore=306`) into actionable backlog | DONE | 11.418 / 615 | Analyzed `docs/compatibility/compatibility-report.md` and raw external logs from `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/compatibility-report-20260310-075152/redis-runtest-external-full`. Collapsed the remaining `err=66` into 11 actionable buckets: `zset` parser/algebra/routing (`27` total), `slowlog` (`14`), `hash-field-expire` error-text/parser (`10`), `hash` encoding/admin (`4`), `string` LCS + `MEMORY USAGE` (`4`), plus `replybufsize`, `scan`, `sort`, `list-3`, and `list` residuals. Also classified `ignore=306` into `Not supported on external server` (`257`), `Not supported on singledb` (`21`), `large memory flag not provided` (`19`), and a small tail of special harness skips. Added dedicated TODO tables below for both `err` and `ignore` lanes, plus explicit note that isolated `unit/scripting` (`94`) and `unit/querybuf` (`4`) remain out-of-band from the `err=66` full-run count. Docs-only iteration; no new validation rerun. |
| 11.438 / 11.439 | Close isolated scripting `unpack` and `min-replicas` residuals with exact regressions and targeted isolated rerun | DONE | 11.437 | Bundled these two residuals intentionally because both live in the same isolated `unit/scripting` lane and share the expensive Lua/runtime rebuild, isolated rerun, and smoke benchmark cycle. Patched vendored `lua-src` through workspace `[patch.crates-io]` and raised `LUAI_MAXCSTACK` from `8000` to `32768`, which makes Redis' `redis.call('lpush', 'l', unpack(a))` workload with `7999` arguments succeed instead of failing on Lua's C stack ceiling. Added exact regression `scripting_massive_unpack_arguments_match_external_scenario_for_eval_and_function` for both `EVAL` and `FCALL`, keeping the existing safe-range `unpack` guard for invalid-index overflow protection. In parallel, wired direct-command `min-replicas-to-write` gating into `connection_handler` and aligned scripting-specific behavior: shebang scripts require good replicas unless flagged `no-writes`, non-shebang `EVAL` only gates on mutating bodies, and `FCALL` consults loaded function read-only metadata. Added exact TCP regression `scripting_min_replicas_gate_matches_external_scenario`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS; targeted external PASS for `Script check unpack with massive arguments` and `not enough good replicas`; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`532 + 23 + 1`); isolated `unit/scripting` rerun improved from `ok=292 err=18 ignore=26` to `ok=295 err=15 ignore=26` at `/tmp/garnet-scripting-isolated-1773152536`. Smoke benchmark artifacts are recorded under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-10/11.438-439-scripting-unpack-min-replicas/`; results were mixed/noisy across `RUNS=1`, `RUNS=3`, and `RUNS=3 REQUESTS=5000`, with no stable hot-path regression signal. |
| 11.440 | Close isolated scripting argv-rewrite replication residuals with exact regressions and targeted external probes | DONE | 11.438 / 11.439 | Ported the remaining Redis `tests/unit/scripting.tcl` argv-rewrite replication cases into exact TCP regressions first: `sync_replication_stream_rewrites_script_spop_commands_like_external_scenario` and `sync_replication_stream_rewrites_script_expire_and_argv_expansion_like_external_scenarios`. Replaced top-level script replication fallback for `EVAL*`/`FCALL*` with a per-request `ScriptReplicationEffect` queue on `RequestProcessor`: each successful non-error inner `redis.call`/`redis.pcall` records the original command id, encoded argv, and RESP reply, and the connection path now emits replicated effects from that queue instead of replicating the outer script command. Added script-specific rewrite semantics so Redis scripting matches exactly: script `SPOP` replicates lowercase `srem` without collapsing the last removal into `DEL`, script expire-family commands rewrite to lowercase `pexpireat` or `del` based on post-command key existence, and script `INCRBYFLOAT` rewrites to lowercase `set key <value> KEEPTTL`. Also kept `FUNCTION LOAD` on the existing top-level path by narrowing effect-based replication to `EVAL*`/`FCALL*` only. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server sync_replication_stream_rewrites_script_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server replicaof_replication_propagates_function_load_and_fcall -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`534 + 23 + 1`); targeted external PASS for `SPOP: We can call scripts rewriting client->argv from Lua`, `EXPIRE: We can call scripts rewriting client->argv from Lua`, and `INCRBYFLOAT: We can call scripts expanding client->argv from Lua` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-scripting-argv-rewrite-20260310-235339/`; smoke benchmark artifacts under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-10/11.440-scripting-argv-rewrite/` show no regression signal (`SET ops +5.78%`, `GET ops +9.92%`, `SET p99 -13.45%`, `GET p99 -11.19%` in the `RUNS=1 THREADS=1 CONNS=4 REQUESTS=2000 PRELOAD_REQUESTS=2000 PIPELINE=1 SIZE_RANGE=1-64` smoke profile). |
| 11.441 | Close isolated scripting frozen-time / expired-restore race with exact regressions, TLA+, and isolated rerun | DONE | 11.440 | Fixed the actual race between script/function execution and background active expiration. Added processor-scoped frozen-time plumbing (`CurrentProcessorScope`, `ScriptTimeSnapshot`, `current_unix_time_micros`, `current_instant`) so `TIME`, expiration calculations, and `RESTORE ABSTTL` inside `EVAL`/`FCALL` use a single cached timestamp across the whole script. Added `with_expired_data_access` and DEBUG-only expired-data reads for `DEBUG OBJECT`, while preserving `ERR no such key` for truly missing keys. The concurrency fix is in `server_runtime`: active-expire ticks now skip while any script/function critical section is running, preventing background deletion of keys restored with frozen deadlines before the script returns. Exact regressions were added first for both request-lifecycle and TCP paths: `scripting_freezes_key_expiration_during_execution_matches_external_scenario`, `scripting_restore_expired_keys_with_expiration_time_matches_external_scenario`, `scripting_function_freezes_key_expiration_during_execution_like_external_scenario`, `scripting_function_restore_expired_keys_with_expiration_time_like_external_scenario`, plus deterministic multishard race regressions `scripting_function_freezes_expiration_while_active_expire_runs` and `scripting_function_restore_keeps_frozen_ttl_under_active_expire`. Formal model `formal/tla/specs/ScriptActiveExpireFreeze.tla` captures the race: `ScriptActiveExpireFreeze_bug.cfg` shows the expected invariant violation and `ScriptActiveExpireFreeze_fixed.cfg` passes. Validation: direct Tcl repro against release `garnet-server` now returns equal positive `PEXPIRETIME` values for both `FCALL` and `EVAL`; `make fmt`; TLC bug/fixed runs as expected; `make test-server` PASS (`545 + 23 + 1`); targeted external single-case probes for `Script block the time during execution` and `RESTORE expired keys with expiration time` are currently harness-skipped in external-server mode (no failures, `ignore=14`); isolated rerun `RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/scripting ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` improved `unit/scripting` from `ok=295 err=15 ignore=26` to `ok=307 err=2 ignore=26` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-scripting-isolated-11.441-20260311-005530/`, with both frozen-time cases `[ok]` and only `Script ACL check` remaining. Smoke benchmark artifacts under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.441-scripting-active-expire-freeze/` show no regression on a 3-run median (`SET ops +0.01%`, `GET ops +3.60%`, `SET p99 -6.72%`, `GET p99 -13.71%`). |
| 11.442 | Close isolated scripting `redis.acl_check_cmd()` residuals and zero the `unit/scripting` lane | DONE | 11.441 | Closed the final isolated scripting bucket by implementing minimal live ACL profile evaluation for `redis.acl_check_cmd()`. `ServerMetrics` now stores per-user ACL profiles (enabled bit, allowed commands, key patterns, `+@all`) instead of only a user-name set. `ACL SETUSER` side effects in `connection_handler` parse the subset needed by Redis `tests/unit/scripting.tcl` (`on|off`, `+cmd`, `+@all`, `-cmd`, `-@all`, `~pattern`, `resetkeys`) and persist those profiles for authenticated clients. The scripting runtime now exposes `redis.acl_check_cmd(...)` in both `EVAL` and `FCALL` execution environments, validates the target command, and checks command/key permission for the current authenticated client user using the live profile. Exact TCP regression `scripting_acl_check_cmd_matches_external_scenario_for_eval_and_function` mirrors the Redis scenario for permission granted, unauthorized command, unauthorized key, and invalid command. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server scripting_acl_check_cmd_matches_external_scenario_for_eval_and_function -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`546 + 23 + 1`); isolated rerun `RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/scripting ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` now reports `ok=309 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=26` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-scripting-isolated-11.442-20260311-011334/`; smoke benchmark artifacts under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.442-scripting-acl-check/` show a small non-hot-path delta on the 3-run median (`SET ops -2.10%`, `GET ops +1.62%`, `SET p99 +7.21%`, `GET p99 -5.59%`), acceptable for this scripting-only command-path change. This zeroes the isolated `unit/scripting` lane. |

Added exact TCP regression `subscribed_mode_resp2_after_hello2_rejects_regular_commands_like_external_redis_cli_scenario` covering `HELLO 3` subscribe flow, publish delivery, `HELLO 2`, RESP2 subscribed-mode error on `GET`, pubsub-style `PING`, and `RESET` exit. Added exact regressions: request-lifecycle coverage for the `COMMAND DOCS` error path and semver-bearing `INFO` / `LOLWUT`, plus TCP integration `redis_cli_hint_suite_matches_external_scenarios_when_repo_cli_is_available` covering `latest server`, `no server`, and `old server` with the upstream `tests/assets/test_cli_hint_suite.txt`. Harness side: `garnet-rs/tests/interop/redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` now tries `make redis-cli -j4` in `REDIS_REPO_ROOT` when the repo `src/redis-cli` is absent or not runnable, before falling back to a native system `redis-cli`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`548 + 23 + 1`); direct exact hint-suite probes saved in `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.445-redis-cli-hint-fallback/artifacts/` all report `SUCCESS: 69/69 passed` for `latest`, `no server`, and `old server`; interop harness smoke with the repo `redis-cli` binary temporarily removed forced the new auto-build path and completed at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-redis-cli-harness-smoke-11.445-20260311-020742/`; smoke benchmark artifacts under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.445-redis-cli-hint-fallback/` show `SET ops -1.26%`, `GET ops -0.18%`, `SET p99 +0.00%`, `GET p99 +29.63%` (absolute `0.135ms -> 0.175ms`), acceptable because the behavior change lives on admin/introspection paths, not steady-state GET/SET. |
| 11.443 | Unblock `integration/redis-cli` external coverage by teaching the harness to use a runnable native `redis-cli` when the repo binary is cross-compiled | DONE | 11.442 | The remaining full-run timeout (`Interactive CLI: should find first search result`) was traced to the Redis upstream test harness itself: `tests/support/cli.tcl` hardcoded `${REDIS_REPO_ROOT}/src/redis-cli`, but in this workspace that binary is a Linux `ELF 64-bit` executable and cannot run on macOS (`exec format error`). `garnet-rs/tests/interop/redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` now resolves a runnable CLI (`REDIS_CLI_OVERRIDE_BIN` -> repo `src/redis-cli` if runnable -> native `$(command -v redis-cli)` fallback), installs a temporary patch into Redis `tests/support/cli.tcl` to honor the resolved override for both TCP and unix-socket calls, and restores the file on cleanup. Note: single-case `--only` probes are not representative for these Tcl cases because Redis `unit/type/zset.tcl` reuses prior setup state; file-level targeted probe is the correct validation unit here. |

Added exact request-lifecycle regression `zset_combine_validation_matches_external_scenarios` to pin the Tcl cases before changing behavior. Remaining `R-002` scope is now `ZINCRBY` hex float acceptance, invalid lex range specifiers, `ZRANGE`/`ZRANGESTORE` syntax + `BYSCORE REV LIMIT`/`BYLEX`, and `zset score double range`. |
| 11.422 | Close the remaining `R-002` zset parser/formatting residuals (`hex float`, lex-range diagnostics, generic `ZRANGE`, score rendering) | DONE | 11.421 | Completed the rest of the `unit/type/zset` parser/formatting bucket. Added exact regressions first: `zset_range_parser_and_hex_float_match_external_scenarios` and `zset_score_double_range_matches_external_scenario`. `parse_f64_ascii_allow_non_finite` now accepts C99-style hexadecimal float literals (`0x1p+0`) via an explicit hex parser; `ZRANGEBYLEX`/`ZREVRANGEBYLEX` and generic `ZRANGE ... BYLEX` now emit Redis-compatible `ERR min or max not valid string range item`; generic `ZRANGE`/`ZRANGESTORE` now parse the Valkey/Redis option grammar (`BYSCORE|BYLEX`, `REV`, `WITHSCORES`, `LIMIT`) with the correct conflict handling; and zset score replies now use a Redis-style double formatter instead of `f64::to_string()`, fixing `zset score double range` and other RESP2/RESP3 score rendering edge cases. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server zset_range_parser_and_hex_float_match_external_scenarios -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server zset_score_double_range_matches_external_scenario -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`511 + 23 + 1`); targeted external `RUNTEXT_EXTRA_ARGS='--single unit/type/zset' ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-zset-r002b-20260310-112824/` reports `ok=324 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=0`; milestone full compatibility rerun at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/compatibility-report-20260310-112934/redis-runtest-external-full` reduces the full snapshot to `ok=2170 err=37 timeout=1 ignore=306`, with `unit/type/zset` contributing no residual failures. Then fixed slowlog semantics by (1) removing non-blocking double logging from the connection path so slowlog is recorded only once per command, (2) moving threshold/max-len checks to record time so `CONFIG SET slowlog-log-slower-than -1` and related self-config commands follow Redis current-config semantics, (3) allowing blocking commands to be logged exactly once after unblock instead of being globally skipped, (4) routing `ACL SETUSER` through the generic processor path and applying metrics registration as a post-success side effect so slowlog/commandstats see the original command, and (5) logging the `MIGRATE -> +NOKEY` external fast path through slowlog with the original/redacted arguments. | 11.432 | Close `R-005` string residuals (`LCS IDX` decomposition, `MEMORY USAGE - STRINGS`) with exact regressions and targeted external validation | DONE | 11.431 | Ported the remaining Redis `tests/unit/type/string.tcl` residuals into exact request-lifecycle regressions first: `lcs_idx_and_matchlen_match_external_string_scenarios` and `memory_usage_string_bounds_match_external_scenario`. Replaced the forward-trace LCS decomposition with a Valkey-style 1-indexed DP table plus backward range emission so `LCS ... IDX [WITHMATCHLEN] [MINMATCHLEN]` now emits the same tail-to-head match segments as Redis for the viral RNA fixture. Also split string memory estimation from generic object payload estimation: `MEMORY USAGE` and total-used-memory accounting now use a string-specific header-size floor (`12B` on 32-bit, `16B` on 64-bit) instead of a flat `64B` entry overhead, which brings small-string results back inside Redis' tested lower/upper bounds while leaving object estimates unchanged. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server lcs_idx_and_matchlen_match_external_string_scenarios -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server memory_usage_string_bounds_match_external_scenario -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`526 + 23 + 1`); targeted external file probe `RUNTEXT_EXTRA_ARGS='--single unit/type/string' ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-string-r005-20260310-1/` with `ok=175 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=0`. Smoke benchmark artifact stored at `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-10/11.432-string-lcs-memory-r005/` comparing base `6467db7f54` vs current worktree (`RUNS=1 THREADS=1 CONNS=4 REQUESTS=2000 PIPELINE=1 SIZE_RANGE=1-64`): `SET ops +1.73%`, `GET ops -8.33%`, `SET p99 -6.72%`, `GET p99 +31.07%`; because this touches non-hot string introspection/LCS paths and is a single-run smoke, treat it only as a guardrail and rerun if this area grows further. |
| 11.433 | Close `R-009` list-3 storage residual with exact external workload regression and larger object-store page-size floor | DONE | 11.432 | Ported the failing Redis `tests/unit/type/list-3.tcl` `ziplist implementation: value encoding and backlink` workload into exact request-lifecycle regression `list3_ziplist_value_encoding_and_backlink_external_scenario_runs_without_storage_failure`. The test mirrors the external scenario shape: `list-max-listpack-size 16`, 200 mixed integer/large-string `RPUSH` values, and full backward `LINDEX` verification, while using a deterministic value stream and external-like Tsavorite page budget (`max_in_memory_pages=16384`) so it stresses the same monolithic list-object persistence path as the external harness. The root cause was the current object model serializing each list value set as one record while the default object-store page-size floor was only `1 MiB`; the list-3 backlink workload crosses that threshold and tripped record-size failures. Fixed by raising `DEFAULT_OBJECT_STORE_PAGE_SIZE_BITS` from `20` to `22` and by refactoring `RequestProcessor` initialization so tests can build processors with explicit Tsavorite config overrides without mutating global env. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server list3_ziplist_value_encoding_and_backlink_external_scenario_runs_without_storage_failure -- --nocapture`, plus existing `list_quicklist_to_listpack_encoding_conversion_matches_external_scenario` regression still PASS); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`527 + 23 + 1`); targeted external `RUNTEXT_EXTRA_ARGS='--single unit/type/list-3' ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-list3-r009-fix-20260310-1/` with `ok=11 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=0`. Smoke benchmark artifact stored at `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-10/11.433-list3-object-page-r009/` comparing base `fb37d1a646` vs current worktree (`RUNS=1 THREADS=1 CONNS=4 REQUESTS=2000 PIPELINE=1 SIZE_RANGE=1-64`): `SET ops -1.26%`, `GET ops +4.10%`, `SET p99 +0.00%`, `GET p99 -23.95%`; as with other one-run smokes, treat it only as a non-regression guardrail. |

| 11.453 | Close the external MultiDB `unit/multi` slice by making `WATCH` / `EXEC` / `SELECT` / lazy-expire / `FLUSH*` / `SWAPDB` semantics DB-aware | DONE | 11.452 | Finished the next `I-002` enablement slice exposed by `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0 RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/multi`. `WATCH` state is now `{db,key,version}` scoped instead of raw-key scoped, `WATCH` / `EXEC` refresh/version checks explicitly run in the watched DB, and lazy-expire replication now carries `DbScopedKey` so synthetic `DEL` propagation can emit the right `SELECT` even when expirations come from auxiliary DBs. `MULTI/EXEC` now tracks per-item selected DB plus final selected DB: queued `SELECT` inside a transaction updates subsequent command execution DB, replication emits `MULTI`, interleaved `SELECT`, write frames, `EXEC` in Redis order, and the client remains on the final DB after `EXEC`. Auxiliary-DB `FLUSHDB` / `FLUSHALL` were rewritten from whole-map drops to per-key semantic deletes so watched-key invalidation matches Redis for existing, missing, and stale keys; background active expire now also sweeps auxiliary DBs; `INCR`/`DECR`/`INCRBY`/`DECRBY`/`INCRBYFLOAT` now operate correctly in nonzero DBs instead of silently falling back to db0. Exact external regressions were added first as TCP integration: `multidb_watch_flush_swapdb_expire_and_discard_match_external_multi_scenarios` and `sync_replication_stream_multi_exec_with_selects_matches_external_multi_scenario`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS for both exact tests; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`561 passed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); targeted external PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-multi-multidb-11.453-fix-20260311-115648/` with `unit/multi ok=60 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=3`. Benchmark artifact stored at `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.453-multidb-watch-transaction-slice/`: one-run smoke showed `SET ops -8.87%`, `GET ops -3.89%`, but `RUNS=3` rerun was noisy/ambiguous (`SET +0.27%`, `GET +199%` on a base outlier), so keep this slice with explicit note that the benchmark does not show a stable reproducible DB0 hot-path regression signal yet. |
| 11.454 | Revalidate no-singledb isolated lanes after `11.453` and continue MultiDB enablement only from real residuals | DONE | 11.453 | Closed the first real post-`11.453` no-singledb residual by fixing `INFO KEYSIZES` to always compute the default histogram under `db0`, independent of the current client `SELECT` state. Root cause: the command was reading `string_keys_snapshot()` / `object_keys_snapshot()` under the caller's selected DB, so a client on `db1` mislabeled `db1` data as `db0` and then counted the same keys again under the auxiliary-db pass. Added exact regressions first in request-lifecycle (`info_keysizes_keeps_db0_histogram_when_current_client_selected_db_is_nonzero`) and TCP integration (`multidb_info_keysizes_swapdb_and_debug_reload_match_external_scenarios_over_tcp`) to mirror Redis `tests/unit/info-keysizes.tcl` `Test SWAPDB` and `DEBUG RELOAD reset keysizes`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`563 passed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); external `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0 RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/info-keysizes` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-121557/` with `ok=18 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=8`. Smoke benchmark artifact: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.454-multidb-info-keysizes-selecteddb/` comparing base `a334a5b760` vs working tree (`RUNS=1 THREADS=4 CONNS=8 REQUESTS=3000 PRELOAD_REQUESTS=3000 PIPELINE=1 SIZE_RANGE=1-64`): `SET ops +18.01%`, `GET ops +6.92%`, `SET p99 -27.20%`, `GET p99 -11.89%`; treat this as a guardrail only because the slice touches admin/introspection paths, not write/read hot paths. |
| 11.455 | Revalidate the next no-singledb bucket after `11.454` and close the first real MultiDB residual in `unit/type/list` or the next exposed lane | DONE | 11.454 | Closed the first real post-`11.454` MultiDB list residual by making blocking wait registration and readiness DB-aware. Root cause: external no-singledb `Linked LMOVEs` uses `redis_deferring_client` on `SELECT 9`, but `BlockingWaitKey` only stored raw keys and `yield_for_blocking_progress -> has_ready_blocking_waiters()` probed readiness under the ambient default DB, so producer `RPUSH` ACK could leak before blocked `BLMOVE` waiters in db9 became visible. Fixed this by extending `BlockingWaitKey` with `DbName`, threading `selected_db` through list/zset/stream blocking wait-key construction, and evaluating blocking readiness via `with_selected_db(wait_key.db(), ...)` so wake visibility matches the waiter's DB. Also tightened blocked-client publication order so `blocked_clients` is incremented only after queue registration and decremented only after cleanup, backed by new TLA+ spec `formal/tla/specs/BlockingCountVisibility.tla` (`bug` counterexample and `fixed` pass configs). Exact regressions: DB9 TCP integration `linked_blmove_chain_is_observable_without_intermediate_residue` now mirrors the no-singledb external scenario, and connection-handler unit `xreadgroup_claim_blocking_wait_detection_supports_claim_and_multiple_streams` now asserts DB-scoped wait keys. Validation: TLC bug/fixed behaved as expected; targeted Rust PASS; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`563 passed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); targeted external single-case PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-124756/` (`Linked LMOVEs ok=1 err=0`); isolated external `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0 RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/type/list` improved from `ok=107 err=7 timeout=1 ignore=9` to `ok=112 err=2 timeout=1 ignore=9` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-124817/`, eliminating `Linked LMOVEs`, `Circular BRPOPLPUSH`, `BRPOPLPUSH timeout`, `SWAPDB awakes blocked client`, and `BLPOP unblock but the key is expired and then block again - reprocessing command`. Benchmark evidence was intentionally not used as a decision gate in this slice because the machine was under concurrent user workload; a noisy smoke run was captured only as a reference under `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.455-multidb-blocking-wait-db-scope/`. |
| 11.456 | Close the blocked-list expired-key replication semantics residuals in no-singledb `unit/type/list` (`SWAPDB` / `MULTI+EXEC` lanes) | DONE | 11.455 | Closed the remaining no-singledb list replication-order residuals with exact TCP regressions first: `sync_replication_stream_swapdb_preserves_expire_then_del_for_expired_blocked_list_key` and `sync_replication_stream_exec_preserves_expire_then_del_for_expired_blocked_list_key`. Root causes split in two: (1) replication rewrite helpers (`SET`/`EXPIRE`/`GETEX`/`RESTORE`/script expire) were DB-blind for auxiliary DBs, so nonzero-DB `PEXPIRE` rewrites collapsed to `DEL`; fixed by threading `DbName` through replication-frame rewrite entrypoints and using non-destructive DB-scoped existence/expiration reads. (2) `SWAPDB` snapshot/import path eagerly expired keys and dropped object expirations, so an expired list key was deleted during snapshot instead of being moved with its expiration metadata; fixed by making `snapshot_current_db_entries()` preserve expired object/string entries without lazy deletion and by making migration import keep object expirations. In parallel, blocked wait polling was changed to preflight readiness non-destructively (`blocking_wait_keys_ready`) so expired list keys are no longer deleted by blocked-command reprocessing before Redis would emit the delayed `DEL`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS for both exact TCP tests; `make fmt`; isolated external no-singledb `unit/type/list` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-133716/` with `ok=287 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=9`; `make test-server` PASS (`565 passed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`). |
| 11.457 | Refresh the post-`11.456` full compatibility baseline and re-triage the remaining MultiDB/admin backlog from current HEAD | DONE | 11.456 | Ran the full compatibility workflow after landing the blocked-expire / SWAPDB snapshot fixes. New baseline: main full external `ok=1511 err=1 timeout=1 ignore=163`, isolated `unit/scripting ok=309 err=0 ignore=26`, isolated `unit/querybuf ok=4 err=0 ignore=0`, isolated `unit/other ok=22 err=3 timeout=0 ignore=6`. Main-full residuals are now exactly `RESTORE with ABSTTL in the past` (`unit/dump`) and timeout `XREADGROUP CLAIM with two blocked clients`; isolated `unit/other` residuals remain `BGSAVE`, `Check consistency of different data types after a reload`, and `EXPIRES after a reload (snapshot + append only file rewrite)`, which points to the snapshot/reload lane as the next highest-leverage target. Updated artifact: `docs/compatibility/compatibility-report.md`, result dir `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/compatibility-report-20260311-134110/redis-runtest-external-full/`. |
| 11.458 | Close the remaining main-full `unit/dump` residual (`RESTORE with ABSTTL in the past`) and use it to reduce the shared snapshot/reload backlog | DONE | 11.457 | Closed the main-full `unit/dump` residual and the coupled snapshot/reload backlog together. Added exact regressions first: request-lifecycle `restore_with_absttl_in_the_past_matches_external_dump_scenario`, `debug_reload_preserves_mixed_dataset_digest_like_external_other_scenario`, `expires_survive_debug_reload_and_loadaof_like_external_other_scenario`, plus TCP integration `bgsave_then_debug_reload_preserves_current_value_like_external_other_scenario`. Root causes were shared: `RESTORE`/reload materialized values whose absolute expiration was already in the past; `DEBUG RELOAD` ignored Redis default save-before-reload semantics unless an old snapshot happened to exist; `BGSAVE` never captured a fresh snapshot at all; and debug-reload snapshot collection re-decoded logical string payloads, truncating reloaded string values while also dropping current expiration metadata from `EXPIRE`-updated strings. Fixed by teaching restore/reload to skip materialization for past absolute deadlines, making `DEBUG RELOAD` without `NOSAVE` build and reload the current snapshot directly, capturing a fresh in-memory snapshot on `BGSAVE`, and collecting reload snapshots from logical string values plus live expiration metadata instead of re-decoding stored payload bytes. Validation: targeted Rust PASS for all exact regressions; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`569 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); isolated external PASS for `unit/dump` and `unit/other` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-140656/` with `unit/dump ok=14 err=0 ignore=14` and `unit/other ok=25 err=0 ignore=6`. Benchmarking was intentionally skipped for this slice because the host was under concurrent user workload and the user explicitly waived benchmark gating for this lane. |

| 11.460 | Close the last main-full residual: `XREADGROUP CLAIM with two blocked clients` timeout in `unit/type/stream-cgroups` | DONE | 11.459 | Ported the exact Redis scenario into TCP integration `xreadgroup_claim_with_two_blocked_clients_external_scenario_runs_as_tcp_integration_test`, then fixed the blocking wake path in two places. First, `BlockingXreadgroupWait` now keeps `claim_min_idle_millis`, and `xreadgroup_waiter_is_ready` considers both new entries after `last_delivered_id` and claimable pending entries whose idle time has crossed the `CLAIM` threshold. | 11.461 | Close the remaining RESP3 nil-shape residuals for blocking zset pop timeouts (`BZPOPMIN/BZPOPMAX readraw in RESP3`, `BZMPOP readraw in RESP3`) | DONE | 11.460 | Added exact TCP regression `blocking_zset_pop_timeout_returns_resp3_null_like_external_readraw_scenarios`, then made the blocking timeout/unblock helper protocol-aware so RESP3 connections emit `_` instead of RESP2 `*-1` for blocking zset pop timeouts.
