| 11.462 | Close the last main-full residual: `integration/redis-cli` `Piping raw protocol`, then refresh the exact full compatibility baseline | DONE | 11.461 | Root cause was parser compatibility, not `redis-cli` itself or the Tcl harness. Redis `tests/support/util.tcl` builds RESP with `formatCommand(...)` and then writes each command with `puts`, which appends a lone `\\n` after an already-complete RESP frame. Garnet only ignored empty `\\r\\n` frames, so the raw pipe file reached the server as `...\\r\\n\\n...` and failed with `ERR Protocol error`. Added exact regressions first: `protocol_accepts_tcl_puts_extra_lf_between_resp_commands`, `redis_cli_pipe_raw_protocol_external_scenario_round_trips_all_replies`, and `redis_cli_pipe_mode_matches_external_scenario_when_repo_cli_is_available`. Fixed the connection parser to tolerate lone LF empty separators between RESP frames. Validation: targeted Rust PASS for all three regressions; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`574 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); isolated external `integration/redis-cli` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-154805/` with `ok=51 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=1`; final full compatibility rerun PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/compatibility-report-20260311-155023/redis-runtest-external-full/` with main full external `ok=2262 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=374`, isolated `unit/scripting ok=309 err=0 ignore=26`, `unit/querybuf ok=4 err=0 ignore=0`, `unit/other ok=25 err=0 ignore=6`. Updated artifact: `docs/compatibility/compatibility-report.md`. |
| 11.463 | Rebaseline `I-002` on current HEAD by running external full without `--singledb`, then close the first real no-singledb residual exposed by that run | DONE | 11.462 | Re-ran the external subset with `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0` at current HEAD and kept it out of the default compatibility artifact lane. Baseline result at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-164258/`: main full external `ok=647 err=30 timeout=1 ignore=104`, isolated `unit/scripting ok=311 err=2 ignore=22`, `unit/querybuf ok=4 err=0 ignore=0`, `unit/other ok=21 err=4 ignore=6`. The first real no-singledb slice collapsed to DB-aware string mutation/expiration helpers: `BITFIELD`, `SETBIT`/`BITCOUNT`, `PERSIST`, and `APPEND` were reading nonzero DB state correctly but writing back to DB0 or clearing TTL only in DB0. Added exact TCP regressions first: `multidb_string_mutations_and_persist_match_external_scenarios_over_tcp` and `multidb_setbit_bitfield_noop_do_not_increase_dirty_counter_like_external_scenario`. Implemented `write_string_value_in_current_db` so auxiliary DB string writes preserve payload + expiration without migration-side watch/track side effects, switched `upsert_string_value_with_expiration_unix_millis` to use it, rewrote `APPEND` onto the same DB-aware read/modify/write path, and made `PERSIST` clear expiration through `string_expiration_deadline` / `set_string_expiration_deadline` instead of directly mutating the DB0 expiration map. The follow-up no-op dirty regression came from `connection_handler` mutation detection reading pre-state in DB0; fixed by threading `selected_db` into `pre_string_len_for_mutation_detection`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS for both exact TCP regressions; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`576 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); targeted no-singledb external PASS for `unit/bitfield` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-165752/` (`ok=18 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=1`) and `unit/bitops` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-170240/` (`ok=55 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=2`). The remaining isolated no-singledb residuals after this slice are now outside the direct string-mutation family: `unit/expire` (`Active expire should increment both expired_keys and expired_keys_active`) and `unit/other` (`Check consistency of different data types after a reload`). Benchmarking was intentionally skipped because the host was under concurrent user workload and the user explicitly waived benchmark gating for this lane. |
| 11.464 | Close the remaining no-singledb `unit/expire` residual (`Active expire should increment both expired_keys and expired_keys_active`) and determine whether it is a real MultiDB regression or an existing background-expire bug now exposed by isolated reruns | DONE | 11.463 | Root cause was auxiliary-DB active-expire stats accounting, not a new data-model limit. The exact TCP regression `multidb_active_expire_increments_expired_keys_active_like_external_scenario` was added first and the active-expire path was traced under no-singledb runtime. `expire_stale_auxiliary_keys_in_shard()` was incorrectly calling `record_lazy_expired_keys(1)` per expired key and then `record_active_expired_keys(expired.len())`, which double-counted `expired_keys` for auxiliary active expiration and prevented Redis `unit/expire` from observing the expected `expired_keys == 3` / `expired_keys_active == 3` state. Fixed by removing the lazy counter bump and keeping the single active-expire accounting path. Validation: targeted Rust PASS; `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`577 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); isolated external `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0 RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/expire` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-172232/` with `ok=76 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=3`. The remaining no-singledb residual is now `unit/other` reload consistency. |
| 11.465 | Close the remaining no-singledb `unit/other` reload consistency residual (`Check consistency of different data types after a reload`) and make MultiDB set hot-state visibility match reload/snapshot semantics | DONE | 11.464 | Root cause was not `HEXPIRE` alone but a broader MultiDB set write-back visibility gap exposed by `DEBUG RELOAD`. First, hash-field expiration metadata for auxiliary DB hashes was missing from the custom reload snapshot, so `DEBUG RELOAD` dropped `HEXPIRE`/`HEXPIREAT` state; this was fixed by extending the snapshot codec to `GRSNAP3`, capturing per-hash field expirations, and restoring them on reload. The remaining inconsistency came from set hot-state semantics in auxiliary DBs: dirty set entries could exist in write-back hot state without being reflected in current-db object-key snapshots, so `KEYS`/`DEBUG DIGEST`/reload snapshot enumeration observed a different keyspace from normal set reads. Fixed by (1) materializing dirty set hot entries before snapshot/flush boundaries, (2) including current-db set hot keys in `object_keys_snapshot`, and (3) clearing per-DB set hot state on flush so stale cache entries do not survive `FLUSHDB`/`DEBUG RELOAD`. Added exact TCP regressions `multidb_debug_reload_preserves_mixed_dataset_with_hash_field_expirations_like_external_other_scenario` and `multidb_set_hot_entries_are_visible_to_keys_and_digest_across_debug_reload`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server multidb_ -- --nocapture` -> `11 passed, 0 failed` including the new regressions); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`579 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); isolated external `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0 RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/other` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-175500/` with `ok=25 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=6`. This clears the currently known no-singledb isolated residuals exposed in `unit/expire` and `unit/other`; the next lane is re-baselining the remaining no-singledb matrix from current HEAD. |
| 11.466 | Close the no-singledb isolated scripting residual (`Script delete the expired key`) and make expired-data debug access respect auxiliary DBs | DONE | 11.465 | After the no-singledb rebaseline at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-180229/` exposed `unit/scripting err=2`, the remaining scripting failure reduced to `Script delete the expired key` in `tests/unit/scripting.tcl`. Root cause was that auxiliary-DB string/object existence helpers (`read_string_value`, `key_exists`, `object_key_exists`) always called `expire_key_if_needed()` even when `DEBUG OBJECT` had entered `with_expired_data_access(true)`. This made expired keys in nonzero DBs disappear before debug inspection, while Redis expects `DEBUG OBJECT` to keep them visible when active expire is disabled. Fixed by honoring `allow_expired_data_access()` in the auxiliary-DB read/existence paths. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server scripting_deletes_expired_key_on_access_like_external_scenario -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; isolated external `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0 RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/scripting` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-181029/` with `ok=313 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=22`. The remaining no-singledb backlog from the latest baseline is now concentrated in main-full `unit/expire`, `unit/hyperloglog`, `unit/latency-monitor`, and one keyspace-notification case. |
| 11.467 | Close the no-singledb `unit/hyperloglog` residual by making `STRLEN` / `GETRANGE` honor the selected DB on auxiliary string paths | DONE | 11.466 | Added exact external regressions first in `request_lifecycle/tests.rs`: `hyperloglog_sparse_thresholds_match_external_scenario_in_auxiliary_db`, `hyperloglog_sparse_stress_matches_external_scenario_in_auxiliary_db`, and `hyperloglog_cache_invalidation_matches_external_scenario_in_auxiliary_db`. These reproduced the isolated no-singledb failures directly under `DbName::new(9)`. Root cause was not HyperLogLog math itself but DB selection drift: `PFADD`/`PFCOUNT`/`PFDEBUG` used DB-aware string helpers, while `STRLEN` and `GETRANGE` still read the DB0 string store directly, so Redis `tests/unit/hyperloglog.tcl` saw `PFDEBUG ENCODING` from DB9 but `STRLEN`/dirty-byte reads from DB0. Fixed by routing `STRLEN` and `GETRANGE`/`SUBSTR` through `read_string_value()` / `object_key_exists()` when `selected_db != 0`, preserving the same value/type semantics as other auxiliary-string reads. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server hyperloglog_ -- --nocapture` -> `4 passed, 0 failed`); `make fmt`; isolated external no-singledb PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-193527/` with `unit/hyperloglog ok=28 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=1`. This slice also confirms a broader MultiDB hardening requirement: command invoke/read APIs must stop relying on implicit selected-DB context. |
| 11.468 | Make command invocation DB-explicit and remove helper-based selected-DB indirection from string/object access paths | DONE | 11.467 | First structural slice completed. Introduced `KeyBytes` and `DbKeyRef<'a> { db, key }` alongside existing `DbScopedKey`, added design/inventory doc `docs/compatibility/multidb-db-explicit-interface-design-2026-03-11.md`, and converted core read/existence helpers to require explicit DB-scoped keys: `read_string_value`, `key_exists`, `object_key_exists`, `key_exists_any`, `key_exists_any_without_expiring`, `object_read_raw`, `has_set_hot_entry`, `set_hot_payload_for_object_read`, and `object_read`. Migrated their current call sites, removed the obvious `with_selected_db(... current_request_selected_db())` anti-patterns where the logical `DbName` was already known, and added regression `db_key_ref_reads_are_scoped_by_explicit_db_without_db0_fallback`. This deliberately stops at the first mechanical slice; write/delete/expiration helpers and command invoke context still need the follow-up lane below before implicit selected-DB peeking is fully deleted. |
| 11.469 | Remove helper-based selected-DB indirection from string mutation and TTL-lookup helpers | DONE | 11.468 | Second DB-explicit slice completed. Converted string mutation / TTL-lookup helpers to require explicit `DbKeyRef`: `write_string_value`, `delete_string_value`, `upsert_string_value_for_migration`, `delete_string_key_for_migration`, `expiration_unix_millis`, and `rewrite_existing_value_expiration`. Migrated call sites across string commands, migration, restore/debug-reload, blocking wait readiness, and replication rewrite helpers. This removes ambient selected-DB lookup from the core string mutation/TTL helper layer and makes DB selection explicit at the interface boundary. Added exact regression `db_key_ref_mutations_and_ttl_reads_are_scoped_by_explicit_db_without_db0_fallback` and re-ran the existing TCP exact MultiDB mutation scenario. |
| 11.470 | Continue the DB-explicit lane by removing implicit selected-DB helpers from object mutation / expiration metadata paths and then thread `DbName` through command invoke context | DONE | 11.469 | Third DB-explicit slice completed. Converted object mutation/deletion and set-hot-state writeback helpers to require explicit `DbKeyRef`: `object_upsert_raw`, `object_delete_raw`, `object_upsert`, `object_delete`, `take_set_hot_entry`, `upsert_set_hot_entry`, `with_set_hot_entry`, and `mark_set_hot_entry_dirty`. Migrated call sites across hash/list/set/zset/geo/stream/string commands, migration, restore/debug-reload, and admin paths so object writes no longer rediscover DB through ambient state. Added regression `db_key_ref_object_mutations_are_scoped_by_explicit_db_without_db0_fallback`. Validation: targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server db_key_ref_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server multidb_ -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`585 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`). This leaves the next slice concentrated in expiration metadata setters/snapshots and command invoke plumbing rather than object mutation itself. |

| 11.473 | Continue the DB-explicit lane by deleting the remaining `current_auxiliary_db_name()` storage/helper branches and then threading `DbName` through invoke/reprocessing boundaries | DONE | 11.472 | Sixth DB-explicit slice completed. Deleted `current_auxiliary_db_name()` entirely from `string_store.rs` and replaced the remaining ambient-DB helper branches in `migration.rs`, `server_commands.rs`, `string_commands.rs`, and `string_store.rs` with explicit boundary-local `let selected_db = current_request_selected_db(); if selected_db != DbName::default() { ... }` checks. This removes the last storage/helper API that rewrapped selected DB into an ambient optional helper and drives the peeking metric to `current_auxiliary_db_name(...) = 0`. Remaining structural debt is now concentrated in invoke/reprocessing paths that still rediscover DB through `current_request_selected_db()` instead of carrying `DbName` in boundary-owned values. |
| 11.474 | Continue the DB-explicit lane by threading `DbName` through invoke/reprocessing boundaries and eliminating ambient selected-DB peeks from internal command execution | DONE | 11.473 | Seventh DB-explicit slice completed. Updated AOF replay to carry an explicit `selected_db` across replayed commands and to persist `SELECT` state across frames instead of silently falling back to DB0 on every operation. Added exact regressions `aof_replay::tests::replay_aof_operations_tracks_selected_db_across_select_commands` and `request_lifecycle::tests::execute_in_db_scopes_command_invocation_without_db0_fallback`, plus updated DB-explicit and HLL auxiliary-db regressions to use explicit DB-scoped harness helpers instead of ambient selected-db state. Validation: `cargo check -p garnet-server`; targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server replay_aof_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server execute_in_db_scopes_command_invocation_without_db0_fallback -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server db_key_ref_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server hyperloglog_ -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`589 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`). Remaining structural debt is now the internal/background paths that still rediscover DB from thread-local request context after invoke has already become explicit. |
| 11.475 | Continue the DB-explicit lane by removing residual `current_request_selected_db()` peeks from background/reprocessing/watch-key helpers and making their owned inputs carry `DbName` | DONE | 11.474 | Eighth DB-explicit slice completed. Background/maintenance queues and debug encoding state now carry explicit DB scope instead of rediscovering it from ambient request context: `lazy_expired_keys_for_replication` now enqueues `DbKeyRef`/`DbScopedKey`, script replication effects take explicit `selected_db`, `string_value_len_for_replication` and `key_type_snapshot_for_setkey_overwrite` now require `DbKeyRef`, and set/list/string encoding/debug bookkeeping (`forced_*` maps, set encoding floor, `DEBUG HTSTATS-KEY` state) is keyed by `DbScopedKey` so same-name keys in DB0/DB9 cannot bleed into one another. Active-expire and auxiliary-db lazy-expire paths now propagate/delete against explicit DBs instead of whatever request-local DB happened to be live. Added exact regressions `lazy_expire_replication_queue_preserves_auxiliary_db_name`, `script_replication_effects_preserve_selected_db`, and `db_scoped_encoding_state_does_not_leak_between_databases`, and tightened the existing lazy-expire queue assertion to verify `DbName`. Validation: `cargo check -p garnet-server`; targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server lazy_expire_replication_queue_preserves_auxiliary_db_name -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server script_replication_effects_preserve_selected_db -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p garnet-server db_scoped_encoding_state_does_not_leak_between_databases -- --nocapture`); `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`592 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`). This slice intentionally prioritized semantic scoping over raw grep-count minimization, so `current_request_selected_db(...)` call sites can still rise mechanically where command handlers now construct explicit `DbKeyRef::new(current_request_selected_db(), ...)` at their real boundary. |

This deletes another ambient helper layer while preserving the explicit `watch_key_version_in_db` query API for transaction/watch paths. Added exact regression `watch_versions_are_scoped_by_explicit_db` to prove a DB9 mutation increments the DB9 watch slot without touching DB0 for the same key name. Validation: `make fmt`; targeted Rust PASS (`cargo test -p garnet-server watch_versions_are_scoped_by_explicit_db -- --nocapture`); `make test-server` PASS (`593 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`). Validation: `make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS (`593 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`); benchmark milestone executed with `binary_ab_local.sh` and one Dragonfly differential run (Garnet `set/get` uplift: `+4.36%/+16.88%`, p99 `-6.50%/-28.07%`; Dragonfly throughput ratio set/get: `0.288/0.187`). Artifacts: `docs/performance/experiments/2026-03-11/11.477-keyspace-notify-explicit-scope/`. |
| 11.478 | Write the full MultiDB / `SWAPDB` / command-boundary redesign document before the next structural refactor | DONE | 11.476 | Wrote `docs/compatibility/multidb-db-model-and-command-boundary-redesign-2026-03-11.md` as the architectural target for the next MultiDB rewrite. The new design doc records the current `garnet-rs` problem statement, why `SWAPDB` is currently `O(entry_count + payload_bytes)`, the required `SWAPDB` complexity target, explicit partitioning between logical-DB control-plane state and storage-owned DB runtime state, and the proposed `KeyScoped` / `DbScoped` / `Global` command taxonomy with explicit DB-bound command contexts. It also compares Valkey and original `.NET Garnet`: Valkey uses `client->db` and swaps `serverDb` contents with watcher/blocker fixup, while `.NET Garnet` binds commands to a DB session / `storageApi` and swaps `GarnetDatabase` objects plus session wiring. The doc concludes that `garnet-rs` must separate logical DB identity from physical storage ownership and make `SWAPDB` a logical-to-storage mapping swap rather than snapshot/flush/reimport. Validation: docs-only iteration, no tests run. |

| 11.159 | Canonicalize TTL-related replication frames to Redis absolute-time forms (`SET`/`EXPIRE*`/`GETEX`/`RESTORE`) | DONE | 11.150 | Extended replication-frame rewrite logic to emit Redis-compatible absolute-time frames: `SET ... PXAT`, `PEXPIREAT`, `PERSIST`, `DEL`, and `RESTORE ... ABSTTL` (while preserving lowercase `pxat`/`absttl` token case when originally supplied). Added connection-handler regression `replication_rewrites_set_and_getex_expire_forms`. Validation: `make fmt`; `cargo test -p garnet-server -- --nocapture` (`244 + 23 + 1` pass); Redis `unit/expire` recheck reached `ok=70 err=0 ignore=3` (`result_dir=.../redis-runtest-expire-20260225-011318`). |
| 11.160 | Close first `unit/functions` parity slice (`FUNCTION LIST` shape, `FUNCTION RESTORE` payload/arity errors, `COMMAND GETKEYS FCALL*`) | DONE | 11.151 | Aligned `FUNCTION LIST` to Redis-style function descriptors (`name/description/flags`), carried function descriptions in registry metadata, mapped malformed `FUNCTION RESTORE` payloads to `ERR DUMP payload version or checksum are wrong`, aligned restore wrong-arity message (`unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'restore'`), and implemented `COMMAND GETKEYS` handling for `FCALL`/`FCALL_RO`. Validation: `make fmt`; `cargo test -p garnet-server -- --nocapture` (`246 + 23 + 1` pass); targeted Redis `unit/functions` probe of 9 selected cases passed with count verification (`expected_ok=9, ok=9, err=0`, `result_dir=/tmp/garnet-functions-targeted-20260225-013434`); broader `unit/functions` rerun now shows `ok=32 err=0 ignore=0 timeout=1` with timeout isolated to `FUNCTION - test function kill` (`result_dir=.../redis-runtest-external-20260225-013444`). |

| R-001 | `unit/type/zset` routing/algebra failures | 11 | P0 | HIGH | DONE | Closed in `11.420`. Regular sets now participate in `ZUNIONSTORE`/`ZINTERSTORE`/`ZDIFFSTORE` as Redis-compatible score=`1.0` inputs, and negative-count `ZRANDMEMBER` no longer panics owner threads on capacity overflow. Verified by exact Rust regressions plus targeted external `unit/type/zset` file run; failed list no longer contains the regular-set / `ZRANDMEMBER` / `routing execution failed` cases. |
| R-002 | `unit/type/zset` parser/error-shape residuals | 9 | P0 | MEDIUM | DONE | Closed in `11.422`. Added exact regressions for `ZINCRBY` hex floats, invalid lex range specifiers, generic `ZRANGE`/`ZRANGESTORE` grammar, and `zset score double range`; implemented a dedicated hex float parser, Redis-compatible lex-range diagnostics, Valkey-style generic `ZRANGE` option parsing, and Redis-style score formatting. Targeted external `unit/type/zset` is now `ok=324 err=0`, and the milestone full rerun confirms no remaining `unit/type/zset` failures. |
| R-003 | `unit/slowlog` semantics | 14 | P0 | HIGH | DONE | Closed in `11.423`. Added exact TCP regressions for Redis `unit/slowlog.tcl`, removed the connection-path/non-blocking double log, switched slowlog gating to current-config-at-record-time semantics, restored post-unblock logging for blocking commands, and brought `ACL SETUSER` / `MIGRATE` fast paths back under slowlog visibility. Targeted external `unit/slowlog` now passes with `ok=18 err=0`. |
| R-004 | `unit/type/hash-field-expire` parser/error text | 10 | P1 | MEDIUM | DONE | Closed in `11.424`. Added exact request-lifecycle regressions for the Redis `hash-field-expire.tcl` error surface and backward-compat/stress blocks, then replaced the expire-family parser with a Redis-style one-pass scan that distinguishes `numFields<=0`, multiple condition flags, duplicate `FIELDS`, trailing unknown arguments, and valid backward-compatible ordering. Targeted external `unit/type/hash-field-expire` is now `ok=32 err=0`. |
| R-005 | `unit/type/string` `LCS` + `MEMORY USAGE` | 4 | P1 | HIGH | DONE | Closed in `11.432`. Added exact request-lifecycle regressions for Redis `LCS IDX`/`WITHMATCHLEN`/`MINMATCHLEN` viral RNA fixtures and `MEMORY USAGE - STRINGS` bounds, then replaced the forward LCS decomposition with Valkey-style backward range emission and split string memory estimation from generic object overhead so small strings fall back inside Redis' tested bounds. Targeted external `unit/type/string` now passes with `ok=175 err=0`. |
| R-006 | `unit/type/hash` encoding/admin residuals | 4 | P1 | MEDIUM | DONE | Closed in `11.425`. Added exact regressions for `HINCRBYFLOAT` upgrade threshold + `allow_access_expired`, restored runtime `hash-max-listpack-value` handling in `OBJECT ENCODING`/`DEBUG OBJECT`/`CONFIG`, preserved hash payload insertion order across fast-path mutation and `HGETALL`, implemented `DEBUG SET-ALLOW-ACCESS-EXPIRED`, and added `hash-max-ziplist-*` config aliases used by Redis tests. Targeted external `unit/type/hash` now passes with `ok=81 err=0`. |
| R-007 | `unit/scan` rehash/iteration stability | 1 | P1 | MEDIUM | DONE | Closed in `11.427`. Added exact TCP regression for Redis issue `#4906` and changed the ordered `SSCAN` view to sort integer-like members numerically, which keeps surviving `0..99` reachable after deleting `100+` members mid-iteration. Targeted external `unit/scan` passes with `ok=25 err=0 ignore=1`. |

Targeted external `unit/replybufsize` now passes with `ok=1 err=0`. |
| R-009 | `unit/type/list-3` storage failure under ziplist/backlink stress | 1 | P1 | HIGH | DONE | Closed in `11.433`. Added an exact request-lifecycle regression for the Redis `ziplist implementation: value encoding and backlink` workload and raised the default object-store page-size floor from `1 MiB` to `4 MiB` under the current monolithic object-value model, which allows the large mixed-value list payload to persist without tripping the storage layer. Targeted external `unit/type/list-3` now passes with `ok=11 err=0`. |

Added exact regression for Redis `List quicklist -> listpack encoding conversion` and fixed `LSET` replacement handling so post-shrink quicklists downgrade to `listpack` when the new payload is compact, while pre-existing listpack -> quicklist upgrade behavior and reload hysteresis remain intact. |
| R-011 | `unit/sort` debug-object metadata parity | 1 | P3 | LOW | DONE | Closed in `11.428`. ## Phase 11A: Performance Unknowns (Need DeepResearch)

| ID | Unknown Item | Why It Matters | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| U1 | Which Dragonfly internals are the dominant reason for GET/SET throughput advantage on ARM64 (scheduler/proactor/sharding/data layout)? | Prevents cargo-cult optimization and focuses engineering effort on high-impact areas. | DONE |
| U2 | Which Dragonfly optimizations are portable to `garnet-rs` with acceptable complexity/risk? | Avoids spending time on techniques that conflict with current architecture or maintainability. | DONE |
| U3 | How much of current gap is network/RESP path vs storage engine path vs allocation path? | Determines whether to prioritize server front-end, Tsavorite core, or allocator strategy. | DONE |
| U4 | Which allocator and memory-tuning choices most affect this workload (`1-1024` bytes, high SET warmup)? | Memory behavior often dominates latency tail and throughput under heavy concurrency. | DONE |
| U5 | Which benchmark settings are fair and representative for apples-to-apples comparison (threads, pipeline, affinity, warmup, memory limits)? | Invalid comparison settings can hide real regressions or overstate improvements. | DONE |
| U6 | Can kernel-path cost (`sendto/recvfrom` + wakeups/scheduler) be materially reduced without large architecture regressions, and what is the lowest-risk path (`io_uring`, batching, busy-poll, kernel bypass)? | Current 1-thread hotspots are dominated by kernel + handoff overhead; without a grounded plan we risk expensive, low-yield rewrites. | DONE (`DR-003` received; implementation follow-ups pending) |

## Design Decisions Log

| Date | Decision | Rationale |
|------|----------|-----------|
| 2026-03-16 | Make OIDC/JWT `AUTH` an explicit sentinel flow (`AUTH __OIDC__ <jwt>`) instead of implicit fallback on arbitrary ACL usernames. | Keeps password auth and token auth unambiguous, prevents accidental/hostile interpretation of ordinary usernames as a token mode selector, and lets the validated JWT claim choose the real ACL principal cleanly. |
| 2026-03-16 | For `AUTH`-based OIDC/workload-identity support, prefer a focused JWT/JWK verifier (`jsonwebtoken`) over a full OIDC client stack in the first implementation slice. | Garnet needs inbound bearer-token verification, not browser/client-side relying-party flows. A narrower JWT/JWK surface avoids unnecessary HTTP/discovery/redirect complexity and reduces SSRF/ops burden while still covering issuer/audience/algorithm and JWKS-based validation. |
| 2026-02-21 | Keep command metadata (`CommandId`, name mapping, routing/arity/mutating flags) in one catalog and make dispatch slow path consume it. | Reduces drift when adding commands, shrinks duplicated edits across `command_dispatch` and `command_spec`, and keeps command-surface expansion lower risk. |
| 2026-02-21 | Do not start kernel-path refactor yet; require a dedicated DeepResearch package (`DR-003`) before implementation changes. | 1-thread profiling shows kernel and wakeup overhead is a major component, but “kernel bypass first” is high-risk; we need option-level and architecture-level evidence to prioritize correctly. |
| 2026-02-21 | Keep a top-level `AGENTS.md` as the single quick-entry operational guide and link it from `README.md`. | Ensures contributors/agents can quickly find mandatory workflow rules (tests, perf gates, DeepResearch tracking, reporting) without scanning the full tracker. |
| 2026-02-21 | Trigger DeepResearch at architecture/concurrency/compatibility decision points, and always track request lifecycle in-repo. | Keeps high-risk decisions evidence-based while preventing hidden human-side research backlog. |
| 2026-02-21 | Track all human-owned ChatGPT DeepResearch requests and pending returns directly in `TODO_AND_STATUS.md` (Phase 11B). | Prevents external-research drift and makes “requested but not yet returned” items explicit before implementation planning. |
| 2026-02-20 | Keep `OrderedMutex` on `std::sync::Mutex`; do not switch to `parking_lot` globally without per-workload proof. | The parking_lot swap removed futex symbols but increased scheduler-yield hotspots and reduced GET/SET throughput in measured local runs; lock backend changes must be judged by end-to-end throughput/p99, not single-symbol disappearance. |
| 2026-02-20 | Use median-of-N aggregation as the default interpretation layer for Dockerized Linux differential comparisons. | Single-run Dragonfly/Garnet differentials showed non-trivial noise; codifying a repeated-run wrapper with median summaries reduces the risk of overfitting decisions to one noisy sample. |
| 2026-02-20 | Use FNV-1a for internal string-store shard mapping and reserve `redis_hash_slot` for cluster-slot semantics only. | Internal store sharding does not require Redis cluster CRC16 compatibility; switching to a cheaper deterministic hash removes avoidable slot-hash overhead from GET/SET hot paths while keeping externally visible slot routing behavior unchanged. |
| 2026-02-20 | Enable `TCP_NODELAY` on accepted sockets by default in the Tokio accept loop. | Local GET/SET hotspot captures remained dominated by small-packet send/recv syscalls; enabling Nagle bypass is a low-complexity latency optimization that slightly improved p99 and SET throughput in the latest sample run. |
| 2026-02-20 | Track per-shard expiration entry counts and skip expiration mutex acquisition when the count is zero. | In benchmark workloads that rarely set TTL, unconditional expiration-map locking adds avoidable synchronization overhead on GET/SET/TTL checks; a zero-count fast path preserves semantics while reducing lock traffic. |
| 2026-02-20 | Reuse precomputed shard index in `GET/SET` hot paths for store/expiration/metadata operations. | Repeated slot hashing (`redis_hash_slot`) was visible in user-space hotspots; reusing one computed shard index per command reduces avoidable routing overhead without changing behavior. |
| 2026-02-20 | Set request-processor default hash-index bits to `16` (with env override) for benchmark-representative workloads. | Repeated A/B sweeps (`10` vs `16`) showed consistent median throughput and p99 gains; low default sizing over-concentrated hash-index lookup overhead and distorted differential profiling results. |
| 2026-02-20 | Isolate Dockerized Linux benchmark builds with container-local `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` and explicit Linux `GARNET_BIN` path. | Running Linux builds against a mounted workspace can overwrite host-platform binaries; isolating build output prevents local toolchain breakage while keeping artifacts reproducible. |
| 2026-02-20 | Treat memtier percentile extraction as schema-sensitive and bind `p99` to the explicit table column. | Prior scripts were unintentionally reading `p90` as `p99`; strict column mapping prevents false confidence in latency gates and sweep comparisons. |
| 2026-02-20 | Add `docker_linux_perf_diff_profile.sh` as the default path for Linux `perf` differential capture from macOS and containerized environments. | Native Linux `perf` setup is not always available on local developer machines; containerized execution preserves reproducibility while producing real `perf.data`/report artifacts for Garnet-vs-Dragonfly analysis. |
| 2026-02-20 | Treat benchmark/profiling automation as local-first in this execution environment and keep Docker recovery steps versioned next to benchmark scripts. | This environment cannot execute GitHub Actions directly; a checked-in local runbook reduces downtime from Docker daemon instability and keeps benchmark procedure reproducible. |
| 2026-02-20 | Gate perf regressions using median-of-N memtier runs plus threshold checks, not single-run values. | Shared CI runners are noisy; median-of-N with explicit throughput/p99 thresholds reduces false positives while still catching material regressions. |
| 2026-02-20 | Require one-experiment-one-commit provenance for performance work (with explicit revert commit when rejected) and store per-experiment reports in-repo. | Working-tree-only experiments made before/after hash tracking ambiguous; enforcing commit lineage and standardized report directories improves auditability and reproducibility. |
| 2026-02-20 | Pursue single-process multi-port owner-node architecture first, with multi-process as control benchmark path. | This approach has better deployment acceptability while still enabling explicit lock-free data-path invariants (`1 owner thread = 1 port = 1 logical node`); concerns (background tasks/durability/config coupling) are real but manageable with strict per-node context isolation. |
| 2026-02-20 | For multi-port owner-node bootstrap, run one dedicated OS thread + Tokio `current_thread` runtime per bind address, and use `GARNET_OWNER_NODE_COUNT` for sequential-port expansion from a base bind. | Preserves strict per-port execution context separation inside one process (`1 port -> 1 runtime thread`) while keeping launch UX simple for `N-core => N-port` bring-up without requiring explicit bind lists. |
| 2026-02-20 | Keep in-process multi-port cluster routing opt-in via `GARNET_MULTI_PORT_CLUSTER_MODE`, with deterministic `slot % node_count` ownership for bootstrap only. | Enables immediate `MOVED`-based logical-node behavior across ports without forcing cluster semantics on existing single-node workflows, and keeps the initial bootstrap deterministic before introducing gossip/failover wiring across in-process nodes. |
| 2026-02-20 | For 1-port vs multi-port claims, require load-matched controls (same total offered load) and record failed benchmark attempts as first-class evidence in experiment reports. | Prevents misinterpretation from non-equivalent client pressure or harness issues; stepwise reports with failure notes make performance conclusions auditable and reproducible. |
| 2026-02-20 | Set string-store shard default policy to `2` (or `1` only when owner threads are explicitly `1`) based on matrix sweep medians. | Broader workload data showed shard `2` is the most stable throughput/latency balance across owner-routing modes, while high shard counts still regress due storage-path overhead. |
| 2026-02-20 | Introduce debug/test-only lock-order and sync-point instrumentation in `garnet-server`, and gate new concurrency behavior with deterministic multi-thread tests. | Upcoming owner-thread / sharding changes increase concurrency complexity; deterministic schedule control and lock-order assertions are required to catch regressions before benchmark-only validation. |
| 2026-02-20 | Keep external DeepResearch output and local profiling workflow in-repo with reproducible scripts and explicit artifact paths. | Avoids drift between ad-hoc local notes and repository knowledge; every performance claim now maps to a checked-in document and command path. |
| 2026-02-19 | Track performance unknowns explicitly and maintain a dedicated DeepResearch instruction file in-repo. | Keeps investigation scope auditable and reproducible, and prevents TODO drift into ad-hoc notes outside version control. |
| 2026-02-19 | Standardize socket-level performance checks on Redis official `redis-benchmark`, with auto-download from `download.redis.io` when missing. | Reuses Redis’s de facto standard benchmark tool while making runs reproducible in clean environments without manual pre-install steps. |
| 2026-02-19 | Use a thin wrapper to translate `cache-benchmarks` Garnet CLI flags to `garnet-rs` env-driven startup instead of modifying upstream benchmark code. | Preserves compatibility with upstream `tidwall/cache-benchmarks` workflow while avoiding invasive local forks and keeping repeatability high. |
| 2026-02-18 | Use a pure virtual workspace at `garnet-rs/Cargo.toml` with five member crates and placeholder sources. | Keeps Phase 0 scaffolding minimal while enabling immediate workspace-wide `cargo check`/`cargo test`. |
| 2026-02-18 | Pin `rust-toolchain.toml` to `stable` with `rustfmt` and `clippy`. | Matches Phase 0 requirement and keeps early iterations portable before Linux-specific `io_uring` decisions are needed. |
| 2026-02-18 | Model SpanByte as a packed 4-byte `#[repr(C)]` header plus borrowed serialized views (`SpanByteRef`, `SpanByteRefMut`) instead of a pointer-bearing struct. | Preserves on-disk/in-log layout semantics while avoiding unsafe pointer ownership in early Rust scaffolding. |
| 2026-02-18 | Keep RecordInfo’s canonical Tsavorite bit layout (including `Modified`/`VectorSet`) and place `HasExpiration` in a reclaimed leftover bit for planned Garnet metadata. | Preserves C# parity for existing bits while satisfying planned expiration flag support from the tracker. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement LightEpoch with thread-local slot/depth bookkeeping and RAII `EpochGuard` instead of explicit suspend/resume API on callers. | Matches Rust ownership ergonomics (`let guard = epoch.pin()`) while retaining cache-line-padded entry table and deferred drain semantics. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement HashBucketEntry as `AtomicU64`-backed packed word with standalone pack/unpack helpers plus CAS entrypoint. | Keeps hot-path updates lock-free and allows direct bit-level compatibility with Tsavorite hash index encoding. |
| 2026-02-18 | Represent HashBucket overflow entry as a single atomic word carrying both overflow address bits and latch bits. | Mirrors Tsavorite’s compact overflow+latch encoding while keeping 64-byte one-cache-line bucket size. |
| 2026-02-18 | Use property-based tests (`proptest`) for SpanByte serialized roundtrip behavior in addition to deterministic unit tests. | Catches edge-case payload combinations while preserving explicit regression tests for layout/flag rules. |
| 2026-02-18 | Place Phase 1 hot-path Criterion benchmarks under `tsavorite/benches` instead of workspace root. | The workspace root is virtual (no root package), so benchmark targets must live in an actual crate. |
| 2026-02-18 | Introduce a dedicated `PageAddressSpace` helper for logical address encoding/decoding and reuse it through `PageManager`. | Keeps address math centralized and explicit for later Tail/Head pointer work in Phase 2. |
| 2026-02-18 | Centralize region-boundary pointers in `LogAddressPointers` and expose only monotonic shift operations. | Enforces one-way address movement invariants by construction and simplifies future checkpoint/fuzzy-region logic. |
| 2026-02-18 | Use a `TryLock`-gated page-turn path in `TailAllocator` and return `RetryNow` when another thread is currently handling boundary crossing. | Preserves lock-free fast path while avoiding blocking contention in boundary-turn slow path. |
| 2026-02-18 | Depend on `garnet-common` from `tsavorite` for SpanByte parsing/serialization in record layout helpers. | Avoids duplicate length-prefix parsing logic and keeps SpanByte wire-format semantics centralized. |
| 2026-02-18 | Model page-device interaction as synchronous trait calls with async-compatible method names (`read_async`/`write_async`) in Phase 2. | Keeps early allocator logic deterministic/testable while preserving an API shape that can later map to actual async I/O backends. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement the on-disk read path as a callback-based helper (`read_with_callback`) that synchronously ensures residency before invoking the completion callback. | Preserves the future async completion shape while keeping Phase 2 tests deterministic and easy to validate. |
| 2026-02-18 | Stress-test `TailAllocator` with multi-threaded allocation loops that tolerate `RetryNow` and assert unique logical addresses. | Validates lock-free reservation semantics under contention without introducing unstable timing-dependent assertions. |
| 2026-02-18 | Start hash-index implementation with a fixed-size `HashIndex` table (`Box<[HashBucket]>`) and explicit hash split helpers before CAS insertion logic. | Establishes deterministic bucket/tag addressing invariants first, reducing complexity when adding concurrent `FindOrCreateTag` flows in the next step. |
| 2026-02-18 | Keep `FindOrCreateTag` scoped to primary-bucket CAS insertion for now and return an explicit `BucketFullNeedsOverflow` signal when no free slot exists. | Allows validation of tentative-insert correctness immediately while isolating overflow allocator complexity into task 3.3. |
| 2026-02-18 | Use a dedicated `OverflowBucketAllocator` with fixed-size page chunks and a concurrent free-list (`SegQueue`) for overflow bucket reuse. | Preserves stable logical-address mapping for overflow chains while avoiding per-bucket heap allocation churn under collisions. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement bucket latching directly on the overflow word using bounded CAS spin loops and explicit reader-drain limits. | Mirrors Tsavorite’s transient lock semantics while preventing unbounded waits during exclusive acquisition/promotion. |
| 2026-02-18 | Validate hash-index collision paths with multi-threaded `find_or_create_tag` tests that force overflow-chain growth and concurrent duplicate-tag races. | Provides practical contention coverage ahead of CRUD integration without yet introducing `loom` model-checking overhead. |
| 2026-02-18 | Define `ISessionFunctions` in Rust as a trait with associated marker types (`Reader`/`Writer`/`Comparer`) plus explicit read/upsert/RMW callback contracts. | Establishes a stable generic callback surface before wiring operation pipelines in Phase 4. |
| 2026-02-18 | Introduce a `HybridLogReadAdapter` bridge trait so generic `ISessionFunctions` implementations can consume raw log bytes without coupling read-path logic to a concrete key/value type. | Keeps read traversal generic while allowing zero-copy-ish log parsing to stay inside storage-engine modules. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement Upsert as a two-stage path: attempt mutable in-place rewrite when size is unchanged, otherwise append a new record and CAS-swap the hash-entry head address. | Preserves core Tsavorite mutation semantics while keeping the first Rust implementation tractable before richer in-place growth/filler handling. |
| 2026-02-18 | Model RMW as a dedicated operation module that explicitly checks the address-based fuzzy window before deciding IPU vs copy-update. | Preserves lost-update prevention semantics from Tsavorite while reusing the same append/CAS primitives introduced for Upsert. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement Delete with a dual path: mutable-region in-place tombstone rewrite when callback output keeps value size stable, otherwise append a tombstone record and CAS-swap hash head. | Preserves Tsavorite delete semantics while minimizing extra appends in mutable regions and retaining lock-free head update behavior in immutable regions. |
| 2026-02-18 | Add a `TsavoriteKV<K, V, D>` facade with `TsavoriteSession` that binds callbacks and computes key hashes internally while pinning LightEpoch per operation. | Provides a practical public API boundary above low-level operation modules without sacrificing current callback-driven semantics or epoch coordination. |
| 2026-02-18 | Validate CRUD through both unit-level facade tests and crate-level integration tests (`tests/crud_integration.rs`) including concurrent workflows. | Improves confidence that composed read/upsert/rmw/delete behavior stays correct beyond isolated operation-module tests. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement the initial network server as a shutdown-aware Tokio accept loop (`run_with_shutdown`) with per-connection task spawning and lightweight connection metrics. | Delivers Phase 5.1 functionality with testable lifecycle behavior before layering RESP parsing and command dispatch in later steps. |
| 2026-02-18 | Model `LimitedFixedBufferPool` as fixed power-of-two levels backed by bounded `crossbeam_queue::ArrayQueue<Vec<u8>>` and clear buffers before reuse. | Matches Garnet’s tiered reuse semantics while keeping checkout/return lock-free and preventing stale data leakage across connections. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement RESP command parsing as a zero-copy routine that writes argument `&[u8]` slices into caller-provided storage instead of allocating a vector per request. | Preserves parse-time zero-copy semantics and enables partial-frame handling without introducing heap churn in the hot receive path. |
| 2026-02-18 | Represent `ArgSlice` as a packed 12-byte pointer+length record and expose an unsafe `as_slice` view API for caller-controlled lifetime reconstruction. | Matches the documented compact layout target while keeping unsafe boundaries explicit at the conversion point. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement command-name dispatch with fixed-length ASCII fast-path checks before falling back to a slower lookup branch. | Keeps hot command routing (`GET`/`SET`/`DEL`/`INCR`) branch-light while retaining extensibility for less frequent commands. |
| 2026-02-18 | Build request lifecycle around a shared `RequestProcessor` that combines RESP parsing, command dispatch, Tsavorite-backed storage callbacks, and inline RESP encoding within the connection loop. | Delivers an end-to-end receive-to-send pipeline in Phase 5 while keeping parser/dispatch/storage responsibilities modular and testable. |
| 2026-02-18 | Use property-based tests in RESP parser coverage to approximate fuzz-style input diversity without introducing a separate fuzzing harness yet. | Expands malformed/edge-case surface coverage immediately while keeping CI setup simple for the current phase. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement initial command handlers directly in `RequestProcessor` (GET/SET/DEL/INCR/DECR/PING/ECHO) on top of Tsavorite session callbacks before adding option-rich variants. | Enables immediate end-to-end TCP validation for core command flow while deferring option combinatorics (e.g., SET EX/PX/NX/XX) to dedicated follow-up work. |
| 2026-02-18 | Track SET expirations in a lightweight in-memory deadline map (`HashMap<Vec<u8>, Instant>`) integrated with GET/SET/DEL/INCR request handling. | Enables practical EX/PX option support now while preserving room to migrate to RecordInfo-based expiration metadata in the dedicated expiration phase. |
| 2026-02-18 | Maintain a lightweight key registry for utility command support (notably DBSIZE/INFO) and clean it during deletes/expiry checks. | Provides deterministic utility responses without requiring full hash-index scan APIs at this stage. |
| 2026-02-18 | Run redis-cli compatibility as a real process-level integration test with explicit async timeouts around CLI invocations. | Validates wire-level interoperability while avoiding hanging test processes when external CLI commands stall. |
| 2026-02-18 | Benchmark command handling at the `RequestProcessor` layer with Criterion to isolate command-path overhead from transport variability. | Provides stable, repeatable GET/SET throughput baselines before adding full socket-level benchmarking. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement EXPIRE/TTL/PEXPIRE/PTTL semantics in `RequestProcessor` using the existing in-memory deadline map before RecordInfo-backed expiry metadata is available. | Unlocks wire-compatible expiration command coverage in Phase 6 while keeping metadata migration isolated to the remaining 6.5 work. |
| 2026-02-18 | Run a lightweight periodic expiration sweep (`expire_stale_keys`) in a background Tokio task alongside the accept loop. | Reduces reliance on pure lazy-expiration access paths while preserving the current request-layer deadline map architecture. |
| 2026-02-18 | Add `PERSIST` command support to remove per-key expiration deadlines and surface TTL state transitions (`-1` after persistence). | Completes the practical deadline-lifecycle control path for in-memory expiration management while 6.5 metadata migration remains open. |
| 2026-02-18 | Use `UpsertInfo.user_data` as a lightweight control bit to set/clear `RecordInfo.HasExpiration` in main-store writer callbacks, and rewrite existing values on EXPIRE/PERSIST to synchronize the flag. | Moves expiration metadata ownership into record headers incrementally without yet changing value serialization for embedded expiration timestamps. |
| 2026-02-18 | Store per-record expiration timestamp as an 8-byte unix-millis prefix inside the value payload and centralize encode/decode helpers in `request_lifecycle`. | Provides a concrete in-record expiration timestamp representation compatible with current callback APIs while preserving TTL semantics across read/update flows. |
| 2026-02-18 | Introduce a separate `ObjectSessionFunctions` implementation and second TsavoriteKV instance for object payload storage, with serialized object framing `[type_tag|payload]`. | Establishes the dual-store architecture foundation required for upcoming hash/list/set/sorted-set command work in Phase 7. |
| 2026-02-18 | Represent hash objects as deterministically serialized `BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>>` blobs in the object store and enforce cross-store wrong-type checks for hash commands. | Delivers Phase 7.2 hash semantics quickly while preserving deterministic `HGETALL` output and keeping future object-type expansion straightforward. |
| 2026-02-18 | Represent list objects as length-prefixed serialized `Vec<Vec<u8>>` blobs in the object store and normalize LRANGE indexes server-side. | Keeps list implementation simple and deterministic for LPUSH/RPUSH/LPOP/RPOP/LRANGE while matching Redis negative-index behavior without introducing pointer-based list structures yet. |
| 2026-02-18 | Represent set objects as deterministically serialized `BTreeSet<Vec<u8>>` blobs in the object store and preserve sorted iteration for `SMEMBERS` responses. | Simplifies SADD/SREM/SMEMBERS/SISMEMBER implementation and testability while keeping wire output stable without extra sorting passes. |
| 2026-02-18 | Represent sorted sets as serialized member→score `BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, f64>` payloads and compute `ZRANGE` ordering by `(score, member)` at read time. | Avoids introducing a more complex skiplist structure now while preserving deterministic ordering semantics and straightforward update/removal behavior for ZADD/ZREM/ZSCORE/ZRANGE. |
| 2026-02-18 | Introduce a standalone checkpoint state-machine module with explicit phase transitions and token validation, then expose thin transition APIs on `TsavoriteKV`. | Establishes a testable checkpoint-control foundation first, decoupled from full persistence execution; epoch/safe-epoch gating can layer onto the same state transitions next. |
| 2026-02-18 | Gate checkpoint transition from `IN_PROGRESS` to `WAIT_FLUSH` using `LightEpoch::safe_to_reclaim_epoch` compared against a begin-time barrier epoch. | Preserves explicit checkpoint phase semantics while ensuring wait-flush only proceeds once earlier epoch-protected operations are reclaim-safe. |
| 2026-02-18 | Store AOF records as little-endian `u32` length prefix + raw operation payload and replay them with truncated-tail tolerance. | Keeps writer/reader format simple and sequential while allowing safe recovery from partially-written final records after crashes. |
| 2026-02-18 | Encode AOF operations as full RESP command frames and reuse `RequestProcessor::execute` for replay application. | Avoids introducing a separate operation-decoder path and keeps replay behavior aligned with live command semantics. |
| 2026-02-18 | Track checkpoint/AOF coordination in a dedicated state object (`CheckpointAofCoordinator`) that emits a recovery plan after checkpoint completion. | Separates orchestration state from storage and network layers, making checkpoint-token validation and replay-offset updates testable before full truncation integration. |
| 2026-02-18 | Implement AOF compaction as copy-from-offset into a new file (`compact_aof_file`) instead of in-place prefix truncation. | In-place prefix removal is OS/filesystem dependent and riskier; rewrite-by-suffix is deterministic and aligns with checkpoint-completed replay windows. |
| 2026-02-18 | Validate crash recovery by replaying only compacted AOF tail commands on top of checkpoint-restored state in integration tests. | Confirms the intended recovery contract (`restore checkpoint` + `replay tail`) without introducing a full checkpoint file format implementation in the current phase. |
| 2026-02-19 | Implement transactions as connection-scoped queued RESP frame replay in the TCP request loop. | This keeps queuing/replay localized to protocol handling with minimal disturbance to existing `RequestProcessor` command paths while preserving Redis-style `+QUEUED` and EXEC array replies. |
| 2026-02-19 | Use a fixed-size hash-indexed watch-version map (`Vec<AtomicU64>`) for WATCH conflict checks. | Preserves low-overhead optimistic conflict detection semantics aligned with the design docs and supports concurrent mutation tracking without introducing per-key lock structures yet. |
| 2026-02-19 | Represent cluster hash slots as a compact 3-byte value object (`HashSlot`) and expose copy-on-write `ClusterConfig` updates. | Mirrors the original slot compactness goal while keeping Rust ergonomics safe (no packed-field references) and making config snapshots immutable for readers. |
| 2026-02-19 | Implement gossip sampling as a pure failure-budget round function with injectable selector/attempt callbacks. | Keeps the core failure-budget semantics testable and deterministic in isolation before wiring async networking and connection stores. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `GossipCoordinator` with pluggable `GossipTransport` and deterministic sampling RNG. | Encapsulates per-round gossip orchestration around the failure-budget kernel, enabling transport integration later without changing tested selection/budget semantics. |
| 2026-02-19 | Implement Redis-compatible slot hashing (`CRC16/XMODEM` + hash tags) and explicit route-decision enum. | Creates a deterministic, testable boundary for MOVED/ASK routing decisions before integrating cluster routing into the server command path. |
| 2026-02-19 | Generate redirection strings directly from routing decisions in cluster config helpers. | Keeps MOVED/ASK response formatting centralized and re-usable when wiring cluster routing into the server request path. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add an optional cluster-config hook to the server connection loop and enforce key-level pre-dispatch routing checks. | Enables immediate MOVED/ASK/CLUSTERDOWN behavior in clustered runs without impacting standalone behavior (`cluster_config = None`) and keeps the integration boundary explicit. |
| 2026-02-19 | Perform CROSSSLOT validation for multi-key commands in the server’s cluster pre-dispatch hook. | Prevents incorrectly routing multi-key requests by first-key only and aligns cluster semantics better for DEL/WATCH command shapes. |
| 2026-02-19 | Expose cluster-aware server entrypoints (`run_with_cluster`, `run_with_shutdown_and_cluster_config`) above the listener-level integration hook. | Makes cluster-routing integration usable from application entrypoints without requiring direct listener-level wiring. |
| 2026-02-19 | Implement ASKING as a one-shot bypass for ASK routes in cluster pre-dispatch validation. | Aligns server behavior with Redis cluster migration flow where clients issue ASKING before retrying an ASK-redirection command on the target node. |
| 2026-02-19 | Enforce single-slot consistency across queued commands in MULTI when cluster routing is enabled. | Prevents cross-slot transactions from executing by marking the transaction aborted on conflicting queued slots and returning `EXECABORT` on EXEC. |
| 2026-02-19 | Expand cluster-routing integration tests to include `WATCH CROSSSLOT` and `CLUSTERDOWN` (unbound slot) paths. | Improves confidence that slot-routing behavior handles non-local key edge cases beyond basic MOVED/ASK happy paths. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `GossipEngine` as a stateful wrapper around `GossipCoordinator` + transport. | Provides a reusable per-node gossip runner that tracks logical ticks and round execution state before async network loop wiring. |
| 2026-02-19 | Validate multi-node routing/failover behavior with per-node cluster-config snapshots and runtime config publish updates. | Exercises realistic 3-node MOVED semantics and ownership changes without blocking on full election/replication implementation. |
| 2026-02-19 | Introduce async gossip transport/runner APIs in parallel with sync gossip paths. | Creates an incremental bridge toward `tokio`-driven cluster manager loops while preserving deterministic sync unit tests and existing call sites. |
| 2026-02-19 | Plan replica synchronization mode from a bounded AOF replay window (`replay_start..tail`) with explicit full/incremental plans. | Captures the core replication decision boundary early, so transport/checkpoint streaming code can consume a tested plan object without duplicating offset-window logic. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `ClusterConfig::take_over_slots_from_primary` to perform failover-style slot ownership reassignment to the local worker. | Encapsulates takeover semantics in one copy-on-write config mutation, reducing ad-hoc slot-looping in higher-level failover orchestration code/tests. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `ClusterManager` as a `tokio`-driven wrapper over `AsyncGossipEngine` for interval-based rounds and shutdown handling. | Establishes the async gossip main-loop skeleton now, so future message receive/merge plumbing can attach without reshaping tested round logic. |
| 2026-02-19 | Introduce `ReplicationTransport` and `ReplicationManager::execute_sync` to orchestrate full/incremental replica sync flows. | Provides a single tested entrypoint that applies sync plans to transport operations (checkpoint first for full sync, then AOF streaming), making later socket-backed replication wiring straightforward. |
| 2026-02-19 | Mirror replication sync orchestration with async transport APIs (`AsyncReplicationTransport`, `execute_sync_async`). | Keeps replication plan application logic identical between sync tests and `tokio` runtime paths, reducing divergence before real network transport wiring. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `ChannelReplicationTransport` backed by `tokio::mpsc::UnboundedSender<ReplicationEvent>` for in-memory async replication event emission. | Provides a concrete, testable transport path that exercises async sync orchestration ordering and error propagation before introducing socket-level replication I/O. |
| 2026-02-19 | Merge incoming cluster configs by slot-claim priority (higher owner `config_epoch`, then lexicographically smaller owner node-id) and publish via `ClusterConfigStore::merge_publish`. | Establishes deterministic merge behavior for gossip-applied config updates while preserving copy-on-write snapshots and predictable tie resolution. |
| 2026-02-19 | Extend `ClusterManager` loop with inbound config-update channel processing (`run_with_config_updates`). | Connects periodic gossip rounds and incoming-config merge application in one async control loop before introducing real network message transport handlers. |
| 2026-02-19 | Consume ASKING on the immediate next cluster-mode command regardless of command key-usage. | Matches one-shot semantics more closely by preventing ASK authorization from leaking across intermediate non-key commands (e.g., PING). |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `InMemoryGossipTransport` as a concrete async gossip transport that emits local config snapshots to peer channels. | Provides an executable message-delivery path for gossip integration tests and manager wiring before implementing socket-backed gossip exchange. |
| 2026-02-19 | Validate gossip delivery + config merge end-to-end by coupling `InMemoryGossipTransport` sender rounds with receiver-side `run_with_config_updates`. | Proves that periodic gossip send, channel delivery, and deterministic config merge can work together as one control flow before real network sockets are introduced. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add worker-tracked sync wrappers on `ReplicationManager` (`execute_sync_for_worker*`). | Centralizes use of stored replica offsets when re-syncing the same replica, ensuring natural full→incremental progression without caller-managed offset plumbing. |
| 2026-02-19 | Represent failover as a deterministic `FailoverPlan` that can be applied atomically to a cluster-config snapshot (`ClusterConfig::apply_failover_plan`). | Separates election logic (best replica by offset) from config mutation, enabling consistent slot/role updates and easy integration in both unit and multi-node integration tests. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `FileReplicationTransport` as a concrete replication transport that records checkpoint IDs and streamed AOF start offsets in per-worker files. | Provides a practical filesystem-backed transport path to validate orchestration behavior without waiting on socket replication wiring, while keeping sync/async paths consistent. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `TcpReplicationTransport` as a concrete socket-backed replication transport for checkpoint/AOF-start signaling. | Validates that replication orchestration can drive real TCP I/O (sync + async traits) with per-replica endpoint routing and explicit unknown-peer error handling. |
| 2026-02-19 | Encode gossip snapshots as framed binary cluster-config payloads and add TCP send/read helpers (`TcpGossipTransport`, `read_gossip_snapshot`). | Moves gossip delivery beyond in-memory channels by validating real socket transport + decode paths, while keeping deterministic config merge behavior unchanged. |
| 2026-02-19 | Validate TCP gossip delivery at manager level by bridging socket-received snapshots into `run_with_config_updates`. | Confirms end-to-end compatibility between TCP gossip transport framing and existing merge loop logic without introducing a separate receive-thread architecture yet. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add bulk per-primary replication orchestration (`sync_replicas_of_primary*`) on `ReplicationManager`. | Consolidates multi-replica sync loops behind one API so callers can drive full/incremental sync progression for all replicas of a failed/active primary without manual worker iteration. |
| 2026-02-19 | Introduce `FailoverCoordinator` to atomically execute and publish failover plans once per failed primary. | Centralizes idempotent failover application over `ClusterConfigStore`, preventing duplicate slot-ownership rewrites while keeping plan selection delegated to `ReplicationManager`. |
| 2026-02-19 | Route server multi-node failover integration through `FailoverCoordinator` instead of direct per-test config publishes. | Exercises the same store-level orchestration path intended for runtime failover handling, reducing drift between tests and coordinator-based production wiring. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `ClusterFailoverController` to combine replica pre-sync and failover publish orchestration. | Provides one high-level control path for failure handling (`sync_replicas_of_primary*` + coordinator publish-once semantics), reducing coordination logic duplication at call sites. |
| 2026-02-19 | Track per-round failed/successful gossip workers and add threshold-based `FailureDetector`. | Establishes a concrete failure-signaling primitive that can feed failover orchestration without coupling liveness state to transport implementation details. |
| 2026-02-19 | Extend `ClusterFailoverController` with primary-worker-id batch handlers (`handle_failed_workers*`). | Makes it straightforward to consume worker-id failure signals (e.g., from gossip failure detectors) and trigger failover orchestration only for failed primary nodes. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `ClusterManager::run_with_config_updates_and_failover` to combine gossip rounds, config merges, failure detection, and failover orchestration. | Connects the new failure detector and failover controller into a single async control-loop entrypoint, reducing external glue code for cluster failover simulation/tests. |
| 2026-02-19 | Validate server redirection updates using the integrated cluster-manager failover loop rather than manual config publish steps. | Proves the new end-to-end path (gossip failure signal -> failover orchestration -> config-store publish -> MOVED response change) from cluster control-plane logic down to client-observed server behavior. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `run_with_config_updates_and_failover_report` and explicit failover execution records on `ClusterManager`. | Preserves the simple loop API while exposing deterministic failover-plan execution details (`failed_worker_id`, synchronized replicas, chosen plan) for observability and integration assertions. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `RequestProcessor` live-migration entry APIs and allow server listeners to run on injected processors (`run_listener_with_shutdown_and_cluster_with_processor`). | Enables ASK-stage live data transfer testing and orchestration (`export/import/migrate`) without introducing network-level MIGRATE command handling first. |
| 2026-02-19 | Track object-store keys in a dedicated registry and expose slot-scoped migration helpers (`migration_keys_for_slot`, `migrate_slot_to`). | Enables slot-based live key transfer for both string/object types and keeps `DBSIZE` aligned with total active keyspace across main/object stores. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add explicit slot-migration transition helpers on `ClusterConfig` (`begin_slot_migration_to`, `begin_slot_import_from`, `finalize_slot_migration`). | Encapsulates migration state transitions in typed config APIs, reducing ad-hoc `set_slot_state` usage and making migration lifecycle intent clearer in integration orchestration/tests. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `execute_live_slot_migration` in `garnet-server` to orchestrate source/target config transitions and slot data transfer in one API. | Provides a reusable control-plane + data-plane wiring point for slot migration flows and simplifies higher-level callers/tests that need full migration lifecycle execution. |
| 2026-02-19 | Make `execute_live_slot_migration` iterate batched transfers until the source slot is drained (`moved < max_keys`). | Ensures callers can throttle per-batch copy size without silently leaving residual keys behind when migrating a slot with many keys. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `execute_live_slot_migration_step` to support incremental migration execution and finalize only when slot key discovery reports exhaustion. | Enables time-sliced migration loops to make bounded progress per tick while preserving consistent finalize semantics and slot state transitions. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `run_live_slot_migration_until_complete` as a shutdown-aware async migration runner over `execute_live_slot_migration_step`. | Provides a practical interval-driven migration loop surface for runtime orchestration while preserving bounded per-step transfer controls and deterministic reporting (`batches`, `moved_keys`, `finalized`). |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `run_live_slot_migrations_until_complete` to schedule multiple slots in round-robin order with shutdown handling. | Provides a simple fair scheduler for concurrent slot migrations (one batch per slot per tick cycle) and deterministic per-slot progress reporting without requiring an external migration coordinator yet. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add slot-state discovery helpers and a detected-slot migration runner (`detect_live_slot_migration_slots`, `run_detected_live_slot_migrations_until_complete`). | Lets control-plane code poll current Migrating/Importing state and execute only mutually-agreed slots, reducing bespoke slot-list bookkeeping outside server migration helpers. |
| 2026-02-19 | Validate detected-slot migration runner in a 2-node server E2E flow. | Confirms runtime behavior from detected Migrating/Importing state through data transfer/finalization to client-observed MOVED redirection changes without manual finalize calls in test logic. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `run_detected_live_slot_migrations_until_shutdown` for continuous detected-slot polling with shutdown support. | Provides a long-running control-plane loop that repeatedly picks up newly detected migrations and executes them without requiring external per-cycle orchestration glue. |
| 2026-02-19 | Add `run_cluster_manager_with_config_updates_failover_and_detected_migrations` to co-run failover and detected migration loops under a shared shutdown signal. | Reduces external orchestration glue by exposing a single server-side entrypoint that composes gossip/failover control-plane logic with ongoing detected slot migration execution. |
| 2026-02-19 | Validate composed runner error propagation for both failover and migration paths. | Ensures callers can reliably distinguish wrapped failover-control-plane failures from migration-control-plane failures when using the combined manager+migration API. |

---

## Doc Gaps Discovered

| Doc | Gap Description | Impact | Workaround |
|-----|----------------|--------|------------|
| 02-tsavorite-hybrid-log | Exact concurrent correction protocol for overflowing `fetch_add` reservations (how to avoid transient over-reservation without losing monotonicity) is only described at high level. | Medium: current Rust `TailAllocator` may pessimistically return retries in high contention page-turn windows. | Implemented conservative retry-based page-turn with monotonic tail shifts; flagged for refinement when deeper allocator invariants are documented. |

| 573 | 2026-03-06 | 11.411 | DONE | Closed remaining CLIENT PAUSE transaction/pause-race regressions and stabilized lazy-expire boundary timing: (1) fixed `CLIENT` fast-path so `CLIENT PAUSE` inside `MULTI` is queued/executed at `EXEC`; (2) added pause-blocked blocking-client tracking so `CLIENT UNBLOCK` returns `0` while pause is active and reliably returns `1` immediately after `UNPAUSE`; (3) fixed `RANDOMKEY` under pause to keep paused-expired keys visible and avoid pause-loop failure; (4) aligned pause/session boundary checks to end-millisecond inclusive (`>`/`<=`) to remove edge-timing flakes and avoid read-before-lazy-expire races. Added regressions: `client_pause_inside_multi_is_queued_and_applies_after_exec`, `client_pause_write_keeps_randomkey_visible_until_unpause`, `client_unblock_cannot_interrupt_pause_block_but_works_after_unpause`. `make test-server` PASS（`470 + 23 + 1`）；full compatibility workflow 再実行（`garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/compatibility-report-20260309-175309/redis-runtest-external-full`）で `XREAD last element from non-empty stream`, `XREAD last element from empty stream`, `XREAD last element blocking from empty stream`, `XREAD last element blocking from non-empty stream`, `XREAD last element from multiple streams`, `XREAD last element with count > 1`, `XREAD: read last element after XDEL (issue #13628)`, `XREAD streamID edge (no-blocking)`, `XREAD streamID edge (blocking)` がすべて `[ok]` 化。残件は IDMP / IDMPAUTO / `XCFGSET` とその他 generic stream semantics (`XADD` trim variants, `XRANGE` exclusive ranges, `XADD streamID edge`)。 |
| 601 | 2026-03-09 | 11.418 | IN_PROGRESS | same `unit/type/stream` backlog の second slice として、Redis `tests/unit/type/stream.tcl` の exact `XADD IDs correctly report an error when overflowing` と `XADD streamID edge` を request-lifecycle regression `xadd_id_overflow_and_future_streamid_edge_match_external_stream_scenarios` へ移植した。実装では `resolve_xadd_id` / `next_auto_stream_id` を修正し、`last_generated_id = <future-ms>-UINT64_MAX` の次の `XADD *` を `<future-ms+1>-0` へ進めつつ、`UINT64_MAX-UINT64_MAX` からの `XADD *` は `ERR value is out of range` を返すようにした。これで Redis の future-max streamID edge と overflow error を両立。検証: targeted Rust `cargo test -p garnet-server xadd_id_overflow_and_future_streamid_edge -- --nocapture` PASS；`make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS（`471 + 23 + 1`）；full compatibility workflow 再実行（`garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/compatibility-report-20260309-201321/redis-runtest-external-full`）で `XADD IDs correctly report an error when overflowing` と `XADD streamID edge` がともに `[ok]` 化。full summary は `ok=1865 err=338 timeout=0 ignore=306` で、直前 run から `err` を `340 -> 338` へ削減。残件は `XRANGE exclusive ranges`, `XADD`/`XTRIM` trim-limit / approx semantics, その後に IDMP / IDMPAUTO / `XCFGSET` 大塊。 |

| 652 | 2026-03-11 | 11.456 | DONE | no-singledb `unit/type/list` の最後の blocked-expire replication residual をクローズ。exact TCP regressions `sync_replication_stream_swapdb_preserves_expire_then_del_for_expired_blocked_list_key` と `sync_replication_stream_exec_preserves_expire_then_del_for_expired_blocked_list_key` を先に追加し、`SWAPDB`/`EXEC` で期待される `PEXPIREAT` と delayed `DEL` 順序を固定した。修正は 3 本柱: (1) replication rewrite entry (`replication_frame_for_command` / script-effect path) に `DbName` を通し、auxiliary DB でも non-destructive existence/expiration snapshot で `PEXPIREAT` を組むよう変更、(2) blocked wait polling を non-destructive readiness preflight (`blocking_wait_keys_ready`) に切り替えて、expired list keys を blocked command reprocessing が先に lazy-delete しないよう修正、(3) `SWAPDB` snapshot/import path を見直し、`snapshot_current_db_entries()` が expired object/string entries を lazy-delete せず expiration metadata ごと移送し、`import_migration_entry()` も object expiration を保持するよう修正。補助として scripting replication effects も selected DB を保持するようにした。検証: targeted Rust PASS（上記 2 tests）；`make fmt`; isolated external `RUNTEXT_SINGLEDB=0 RUNTEXT_RUN_ONLY_ISOLATED_UNIT=unit/type/list` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-external-20260311-133716/` with `ok=287 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=9`; `make test-server` PASS（`565 passed, 2 ignored; 23 passed; 1 passed`）。 |

| 618 | 2026-03-10 | 11.421 | DONE | `R-002` の zset combine/parser error-shape slice をクローズ。`parse_zset_numkeys_and_keys` を command-aware にして `numkeys=0` を `ERR at least 1 input key is needed for '<command>' command` へ揃え、`parse_zset_combine_options` の invalid `WEIGHTS` は Redis と同じ `ERR weight value is not a float` に変更した。さらに `ZINTERCARD` の `LIMIT` parser を専用化し、invalid/negative `LIMIT` を generic integer parse ではなく Redis-style `ERR LIMIT can't be negative` family に載せ替えた。exact regression `zset_combine_validation_matches_external_scenarios` を先に追加して Tcl ケースを固定し、検証は targeted Rust PASS、`make fmt`、`make test-server` PASS（`509 + 23 + 1`）、targeted external `RUNTEXT_EXTRA_ARGS='--single unit/type/zset' ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-zset-r002a-20260310-104812/` で `ok=315 err=9`。`failed-tests.txt` から `ZINTERCARD with illegal arguments`, `ZUNIONSTORE with NaN weights`, `ZINTERSTORE with NaN weights`, `zunionInterDiffGenericCommand at least 1 input key` が消え、`R-002` は `ZINCRBY` hex float acceptance / invalid lex range specifiers / `ZRANGE` parser / `zset score double range` の残り 9 件に縮小した。 |

| 624 | 2026-03-10 | 11.427 | DONE | `R-007` SSCAN shrink/rehash residual bucket を targeted-first でクローズ。Redis `tests/unit/scan.tcl` issue `#4906` を exact TCP regression `sscan_shrink_regression_issue_4906_matches_external_scenario` として先に追加し、その repro を基準に set scan ordering を修正した。原因は `SSCAN` が lexicographic `ordered_members()` 上の raw index cursor を使っており、`100+` を削除すると surviving `11` などの integer member が前方へ詰まって未訪問のままスキップされることだった。修正として ordered scan view では canonical integer-like members を数値順、非整数 members をその後ろの bytewise 順で返すようにした。検証: targeted Rust `cargo test -p garnet-server sscan_ -- --nocapture` PASS（`3 passed`）；`make fmt`; `make test-server` PASS（`521 + 23 + 1`）；targeted external `RUNTEXT_EXTRA_ARGS='--single unit/scan' ./redis_runtest_external_subset.sh` PASS at `garnet-rs/tests/interop/results/redis-runtest-scan-r007-20260310-152500/` with `ok=25 err=0 timeout=0 ignore=1`。 |
